entry.title: Re: SAC Planning and Datasphere: How to use Audit Logs for Analysis and Recovery
entry.content:
Hey @Tano_B ,

Really cool post! 

No SAC expert myself, so forgive me if I ask a stupid question or two 🙂 .

  1. In BW, the REQUEST TSN is just a timestamp, although a very detailed one.
    In the Data Change log, can the combination of [Audit Time, Audit User] not be considered a "unique" transaction, similar to a request in an ADSO?
  2. Because we can plan on nodes in a hierarchy, how would you suggest we handle something like rolling back a single transaction? 
    Let's say I delete the numbers from two accounts (by accident) which reduces the total on the above levels in the account hierarchy, and then later someone posts to the top node, which then disaggregates the values. 
    Lets say we know we know our budget must be 1 mio. euros exactly. So the last step is to post 1 mio. to the top node. 
    If I roll back my transaction only, then the amount on top changes.
    Should we then roll back all transactions from my mistake and forward?

Again, just curious! Really love the blog and the idea 🙂

entry.title: Re: AUTO update MIGO date into MIRO baseline date
entry.content:
Dear @Gautam_FI,

In classic MIRO (on-prem / private cloud) the Badi you would use to set Baseline Date (ZFBDT) based on GR/MIGO posting date is:

  • Badi MRM_PAYMENT_TERMS it can change ZFBDT and SAP even describes the example baseline date derived from goods receipt date

However this is a classic MM-IV Badi and is not provided as a released Cloud Badi for key user/developer extensibility in Public Cloud

In Public Cloud apps SAP standard behavior is:

  • baseline date is proposed according to payment terms customizing in CBC SSCUI 102934 Maintain Terms of Payment

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3457995

  • SAP does not describe any released Badi there to switch baseline date logic to GR posting date

So you cannot implement Baseline Date = MIGO/GR posting date via a Public Cloud Badi for normal supplier invoice entry

IMPORTANT:

SAP does provide a Cloud Badi for ERS:

  • MRM_ERS_DATA_MODIFY_CE

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3383434

But that’s only for ERS not for manual PO invoice posting in Create Supplier Invoice / MIRO logic

Practical options in Public Cloud:

1. Configuration only (standard) adjust payment terms baseline date rule in SSCUI 102934 but it won’t give you GR date

2. Process change so use ERS then you have a Cloud Badi available

3. Integration approach post invoices via API/integration where you control baseline date (clean-core), if business can accept that

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Absolute Exchange Rate Not Visible in Open Items in M...
entry.content:
Good day @Jessicaaguilar1 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshots, it matches what the system is expected to do. Below are answers to your questions.

Question: Why is the absolute exchange rate not available as a column in the open items view?

Answer: The short answer is that Manage Supplier Line Items only displays the fields that are exposed by app data source and UI configuration. The app is intended for supplier line item operations (open items, clearing and payment related process) and not all journal entry attributes are available as list columns. You can find more information in Manage Supplier Line Items: FAQ.       

So, even though the Journal Entry object contains the Absolute Exchange Rate as you have in your drilldown (attached screenshot), that filed is not necessarily exposed in the Manage Supplier Line Items table.

Question: Is this the standard behaviour of the app?

Answer: yes, it is a standard behaviour if, the field does not appear in column personalisation, Adapt Filters, and there are no standard settings in the app to enable it. In other words, the filed exists in the accounting document but the app list view is not designed to display it.

Question: Is there any way to display it without having to open each journal entry individually?

Answer: The short answer is no, not if the field is not exposed as a column.

You may consider using a different report focused on journal entries to analyse posting attributes, build a custom report and check key user extensibility. See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/86248754a0cd9d62e10000000a445394.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/16faf6b93de44a4cadce11f9094ef06e.html?locale=en-US data source extensions

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Customer Specific Inventory Reservation based on Bill...
entry.content:
Contracts in SAP (VA41/VA42) are agreements for future sales, not physical stock reservations. They don’t actually block or segregate inventory; they only ensure that sales orders reference the contract. So while they can control pricing/quantities for a specific customer, they cannot reserve stock in the warehouse or prevent other customers from consuming it.

If consignment is not acceptable, your Z‑table + user exit approach is valid, but to simplify:

  • Use storage location control (special storage location for reserved stock).
  • Add a simple enhancement in ATP check (user exit EXIT_SAPLATPC_001) to prioritize reserved stock for the mapped Bill‑to party.
  • For expiry, a batch job can move expired reserved stock back to unrestricted (movement 311). This way, you avoid complex development and keep it lightweight.

The other option is below one

Maintain a variable table (TVARVC) with entries like:

  • Bill‑To Party
  • Material
  • Reserved Quantity
  • Valid From / Valid To

This table acts as the “reservation master.” Business users can update it directly without ABAP changes.

Logic Flow

1) Sales Order Creation (VBAP / VBEP check)

  • When a sales order line is created, trigger a user exit (e.g., USEREXIT_CHECK_VBAP or ATP check exit EXIT_SAPLATPC_001).
  • Read TVARVC for a matching entry:
    • Bill‑To = Sales Order Bill‑To
    • Material = Sales Order Material
    • Validity period covers current date

2) Stock Confirmation

  • If a match is found:
    • Confirm stock from the reserved storage location (dedicated for this scenario).
    • Reduce the reserved quantity in TVARVC (or maintain consumption logic in a Z‑table linked to TVARVC).
  • If reserved quantity is insufficient:
    • Confirm the available reserved stock first.
    • Remaining quantity is confirmed from unrestricted stock.

3) Expiry Handling

  • A background job checks TVARVC daily.
  • If Valid To < today, then:
    • Move any remaining reserved stock back to unrestricted (movement 311).
    • Clear or deactivate the TVARVC entry.

4) Visibility Control

  • For other customers (Bill‑To not matching TVARVC entry):
    • Reserved storage location stock is invisible for ATP.
    • Only unrestricted stock is considered.

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning and Datasphere: How to use Audit Logs for Analysis and Recovery
entry.content:
@CLTGravesen really appreciate your questions!
I would say, to be 100% sure, you should look at the combination of Timestamp, User and Version, because you can only post to a specific version with a single data action to invert postings. When you publish data, you can publish to more than one version at once, so you could have data on different versions at the same timestamp.

Regarding your second question, it denpends:

  • If you never had 1 Million on the correct accounts, you have to 1. undo the disaggregation and 2. undo the deletion of the two accounts and 3. plan the 1 Million afterwards.
  • If you had the 1 Million already planned correctly before, it would be sufficient to undo disaggregation and the deletion

entry.title: Re: Fix pdfmake Table Width Issues with Long Continuous Text and Special Characters (SAP UI5)
entry.content:
Great write-up—this is a classic works in testing, breaks in production scenario.

The key insight here is that it’s not really a column width issue, but a line-breaking limitation. When you feed pdfmake long, continuous strings with no natural breakpoints, the layout engine simply has nowhere to wrap—so it overflows, even with fixed widths.

I especially appreciate that you focused on a practical workaround that preserves the visual output without redesigning the layout. Real-world data always exposes edge cases like this, and documenting them is incredibly valuable.

entry.title: Re: where to see which part is done 309 to other?
entry.content:
Hi @Anshul_-2240 ,
If you’re trying to see material document 309 movements together with the original component/primary part in one screen—without double‑clicking each line in MB51.
This is a very common pain point in SAP PP/MM because MB51 does not show the “reference material” for 309 movements in the main list.

For you the better options to see both materials in one screen, Use MB51 with “Material Document Item” + “Batch” + “Text” + “Movement Type”. Still not perfect, but you can at least group by document number and see both materials together. However, this still does NOT show the “other material” in the same row.

entry.title: Re: Record quantity indicator in center cost
entry.content:
Good day @jantoniomoyano3 

Thank you for your question.

Your question is very generic. Based on your description, what you experienced is expected. I will address your it as follows:

Question: Is there any information about this field or how it works?

Answer: Yes, but it is often not explained in a very visible way in Public Cloud app level help.

In practice, the Record Quantity field in the cost center is a control indicator for quantity recording behaviour. It does not directly control amount posting logic and does not automatically create an amount warning by itself.

The reason why you do not see differences in your posting when the flag is marked or unmarked can be linked to a few factors such as:

  • Your posting scenario is not quantity relevant
  • Your posting account is not set up for quantity recording
  • Your process does not trigger interactive warning checks
  • You are comparing only amounts. This field usually does not change amount postings.

So, the lack of visible difference in your posted documents does not necessarily mean the field is not working.

Regarding the lack of warning about the amount, this is usually not the case, not specifically about the amount. This field is generally related to quantity entry/quantity recording checks, not amount tolerance or amount validation. If your expectation is an amount warning, that is typically controlled elsewhere, not by the by the Record Quantity indicator on the cost center.

You can test it by using a posting scenario that supports quantity entry, use a quantity relevant posting account, post once with quantity and once without and compare the warnings. See the factors mentioned above.

I recommend you verify in your system that:

  • The cost flag is activated in Manage Cost Centers
  • Posting account is quantity relevant
  • Test in posting app that supports manual quantity entry, compare message behaviour and quantity fields in journal entry.

See the following references for more details.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2752349

This Blog provides details: Custom Journal Entry Query by G/L and Cost Center in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Questions and Answers about E-Invoicing and E-Reporting for France in SAP Business One
entry.content:
On December 29, 2023, the French authorities published legislation in the 2024 budget that included updated timelines for the e-invoicing and e-reporting mandate. According to this legislation, the implementation schedule is as follows:

September 1, 2026: All taxpayers will be required to receive e-invoices. Additionally, the issuance of B2B (business-to-business) e-invoices and e-reporting will become mandatory for large enterprises ("GE") and intermediate-sized enterprises ("ETI").

September 1, 2027: The issuance of B2B e-invoices and e-reporting will become mandatory for small and medium-sized enterprises ("PME") and micro companies ("TPE").

SAP is officially registered as an Agreed Platform (PA) by the French tax authorities, the General Directorate of Public Finance (DGFiP). This registration enables customers to seamlessly comply with France's e-invoicing and e-reporting requirements.

In SAP Business One, the functionality that will support the E-Invoicing and E-Reporting in France is under development. Since partners have been asking many questions in advance, this blog post aims to provide clarity and serve as a helpful resource for all partners and customers who may have similar inquiries.

The summary of the questions and answers can be found here.

Q1:

Will the agreed platform (PA), the mandatory third party in France, be SAP?

Answer

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), Cloud Edition, will be supported as an approved platform (PA) by SAP Business One. SAP DRC, Cloud Edition, is a platform approved by the French tax authorities. If customers want to use the end-to-end solution, they will have to subscribe to DRC. DRC will be able to receive electronic documents in all three formats (UBL, CII, and Factur-X) and will transform them into UBL format in order to import these electronic documents into SAP Business One.

Q2:

Regarding imports, will the purchase invoice be created in SAP Business One with links to the goods receipt PO?

Answer

Yes, it is possible to link A/P invoice to either a goods receipt PO or a purchase order.

Q3:

Regarding export, will a wizard be used?

Answer

The Document Generation Wizard can be used for this purpose.

Q4:

Will SAP provide a DRC connector for SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA and SAP Business One SQL?

Answer:

It will be delivered for both. It is universal.

Q5:

How do the connections work between DRC SaaS and SAP Business One On-Premise?

Answer:

This part is under development. All details will be provided once completed.

Q6:

SAP has been registered as PA by French local authorities. How will our customers be able to use/subscribe to this service?

Answer:

Please find the link to the SAP help page where the onboarding process is explained in more detail:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/getting-started-with-integration-of-process

Q7:

What components or configurations must we implement? Is there a specific solution or SAP module to which the customer needs to subscribe?

Answer:

After customers complete onboarding for DRC Cloud Edition, they can activate the process: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/activating-processes?locale=en-US 

They need to integrate Peppol Exchange with SAP Business One: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/integrating-peppol-exchange-with-sap-business-one?locale=en-US&q=France+process  and activate processes for France.

How the connection between DRC and SAP  Business One will work is still under development. All details will be announced once development is complete.

Q8:

The SIREN, SIRET, Routing Code, Suffix are required as part of the electronic address. Where can we find them?

Answer:

The SIREN number is available in Business Partner Master Data → Accounting tab → Tax sub-tab.

SIRET number, Routing Code and Suffix are available in Business Partner Master Data → Address tab → Bill To address.

SAP Business One plans to support basic scenarios. The list can be found here:

Additional mappings need to be made by the relevant partner and/or customer to support other industry-specific or use-case-specific scenarios.

Many of open issues might be clarified in this work zone: https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/CK8AhwChqYjMisy8oYSfgv/workpage_tabs/LxnjcOht6DIhuitgncZLhH 

I hope this Q&A brings more clarity to this topic. Should you have any further questions, please share them in the comment section

entry.title: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer scenario and TM authorizations
entry.content:
Advanced stage building is called for advanced intercompany STO scenario for FU creation in TM whether you activate it on integration profile or not. (rlv. class. /SCMTMS/CL_FUB_STG_BUILD_ADV)

Based on entered incoterm and incoterm location 1, FU stages may be split and relevant organizations (exec org and paying org) can be assigned to stage level. In some scenarios and combinations, both stages could be assigned different involved companies (sender and receiver).

In this type of situation, FU stages may be planned by involved organizations to different capacity documents. It means that FU needs to be available to pass both organizations authorization checks.

Question is; does T_TOR_EXE authorization object checks (in worklists, doc UIs, TC, etc.) only works for root execution organization or does it also check the stage execution organization?

PRD. Version: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 (on premise)

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Blue Green Deployment - Cannot bind "idle" app to BTP Object Store
entry.content:
I'm trying to switch from regular deployments to blue green deployments using the "cf deploy --strategy blue green" setting. This deploys our CAP app twice, once as "live" and once as "idle". The live app is already bound to the BTP Object Store via a service binding. The deployment tries to bind the new "idle" application now to the same Object Store instance as well but fails with the following message:

Binding service instance "object-store" to application "my-backend-idle"...
Async operation for service binding "9920bf33-8100-4aa9-9829-f687e5869ad4" between app "my-backend-idle" and service instance "object-store" 
with offering "objectstore" and plan "standard" failed with "10009 CF-UnableToPerform bind could not be completed: 

Service broker error: Service broker object-storage-broker failed with: 
Cannot create anymore bindings for service instance 0130aae9-1217-41a7-a864-405e2c74cf2c. 
The limit on number of bindings 5, that can be created for each serviceInstance has been reached. 
If more binding credentials are required, please unbind one of your applications or make use of cf service keys concept. 
Details on how to use service keys can be found in user documentation."

A step of the process has failed. Retrying it may solve the issue.
Process failed.

Currently, there is only one application bound to the service instance, and it still fails with the error message that a max of 5 apps can be bound. Even when checking directly with cf curl "/v3/service_credential_bindings?service_instance_guids=<id> I can only see the existing bound app and no other bindings. According to the service documentation, it should be possible to bind 5 apps, so I'm not sure why it thinks the limit is reached here when it's clearly not. Is there any way to "reset" this without having to delete the whole service instance? I already tried removing the existing binding and rebinding, but the second binding always fails with the same message... Any help would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, 2025 FPS01 – What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, 2025 FPS01. With this release, we further expand the Predictive Labor Demand Planning capabilities available for SAP EWM, add in more functionalities and scenarios for the Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration, deliver a host of extensibility and user experience improvements, strengthen our Suite Integration capabilities, and much more.

And unlike many of our past product release blog posts, I would like to begin today’s overview with the latest updates to our AI capabilities in Extended Warehouse Management. To find out more technical details and information on each feature (such as Customization steps) I shall link the corresponding “What’s New” viewer from the help portal in the title of the subchapters.

ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE

With our AI strategy for Logistics and warehousing, we aim to address further on the labor and resource shortage in the warehouse execution. We achieve this via machine learning–based predictive analytics to forecast current and future workloads and conduct forecast based on long-term historical data across warehouse operations, as well as via Joule copilot to provide advanced warehouse operation, and proposing insightful solutions to streamline the outbound process. With this feature pack release, we continue to advance towards this goal via the following updates.

Feature enrichment for AI-assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning

In the previous two product releases, our teams have delivered a solid foundation for Predictive Labor Demand Planning (LDP), through which warehouse operators and shift managers can:

  • Predict planned workload on Outbound Delivery Order and Warehouse Order/Warehouse Task level based on historical data collected from RF Picking and Packing activities
  • Use a minimum of 2 weeks of historical data and predicts the short-term labor demand in next days or weeks
  • Utilize the SAP Smart Business dashboards to gain insights on workload duration, calculated Full-time-equivalents (FTE) and other important aspects such as volume, weight, and number of expected items

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01 release, the solution now supports the persistence of aggregated historical workload data and determines planned duration for outbound delivery staging.

Additionally, we have expanded the range of executed picking workload that can be captured – such as the start date and time - to improve LDP reporting, KPI calculation, and historical workload tracking for warehouse task confirmations via the external API. This has been done by integrating and enabling a two-step pick process for historical records and time tracking using an existing remote-enabled ODATA-API: API_WAREHOUSE_ORDER_TASK_2.

Also, on the topic of reporting for Labor Demand Planning, we now directly integrate the pLDP statistics to the Fiori App “Warehouse KPIs”, providing users with a view of workload forecast KPIs directly as configurable cards, alongside seamless navigation to the detailed smart-business drill-down apps.

The user experience is further enhanced through dynamic date functions. The first forecast graph now displays the total forecast value with drill-down to activity areas for deeper insight. Workload forecasts can be analyzed through five KPI measures—Duration, Volume, Weight, Capacity Consumption, and Number of Items—offering a clear and structured view of operational demand.

Joule integration with the EWM Outbound process

In the upcoming releases of Joule monthly schedule, we will release the full capabilities of Joule feature “Talk to your Warehouse Outbound process”, providing users with the possibility not only to retrieve data from EWM backend on Outbound Delivery Orders, but also to trigger next steps and accelerate the outbound fulfillment process. In addition, users can also navigate directly in the Joule conversation to the corresponding FIORI apps.

Get an overview on all available Joule capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition via our dedicated SAP Help – Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - highlighting transactional and informational capabilities to stay up-to-date.

ADVANCED SHIPPING & RECEIVING

Integration between transportation and warehouse logistics is a significant contributor towards logistics efficiency, synchronizing process changes between warehouse and transportation, reducing overall logistics-costs by optimizing and streamlining freight planning with warehouse execution. To further tighten this integration, with this release we provide the following enhancements and updates to Advanced Shipping & Receiving.

Perform ad-hoc loading

In this latest release, we further streamline the loading process by enabling warehouse operators to assign Outbound Delivery Orders to freight orders during the radio frequency (RF) loading process in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. When the first handling unit (HU) is scanned and loaded, the complete Outbound Delivery Order, including the remaining HUs, is assigned to the Freight Order. Ad-hoc loading can be performed in the following RF transactions:

  • Loading By HU
  • Loading By Door
  • Loading By Bill of Lading

This new process helps to simplify loading in a busy warehouse, in such scenarios as when:

  • There are regular freight orders to a certain plant or region. When the truck arrives, all the HUs in the staging area for this plant or region can be loaded onto it. This procedure is typically used in combination with the immediate loading process.
  • A truck at the door has some space left that can be further filled. Additional suitable HUs can be loaded onto the truck, and the system automatically assigns the corresponding Outbound Delivery Order to the Freight Order.

To see if an Outbound Delivery Order is relevant for Ad-Hoc Loading, users can check in the Outbound Delivery Orders app (transaction /SCWM/PRDO) and/or the Warehouse Monitor app (transaction /SCWM/MON).

Warehouse-Driven Outbound Delivery Order split

In the shipping process, sometimes companies need to split Handling Units based on actual pick, pack and stage status, according to various exceptions in the outbound process. To increase warehouse and shipping flexibility and allow the adjustment of deliveries according to operational reasons, we provide the option to do a warehouse-driven Outbound Delivery Order split with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition FPS01.

With this feature, warehouse and shipping operators can split Outbound Delivery Orders in the warehouse-driven scenario of Advanced Shipping and Receiving triggered from EWM. For example, for an urgent customer order and in case of stock insufficiency, it is now possible to split the Outbound Delivery Order into available quantity and the remaining quantity. So the available quantity can be sent to the customer as planned, and the remaining quantity can be sent at a late point in time, while the corresponding new split-Freight Unit can be considered for further planning in Transportation Management.

 

The split of Outbound Delivery Orders can take place up until the status Ready for Shipping is set. The Outbound Delivery Orders can be split in the following ways:

  • Split Outbound Delivery Order item into a new Outbound Delivery Order – the unpicked (and unpacked) item quantity is split from an Outbound Delivery Order into a new Outbound Delivery Order. For example, there is currently not enough quantity available in the warehouse, but the quantity that has already been picked could be shipped with an available transportation vehicle. The picked quantities stay in the original Outbound Delivery Order, and the unpicked quantities move to a new Outbound Delivery Order, including the corresponding Freight Unit in Transportation Management (TM).
  • Split handling unit (HU) into new Outbound Delivery Order – one or more selected HUs are split into a new Outbound Delivery Order. For example, an HU is to be delivered on a different date in the future. The HU, which is split, is moved to a new Outbound Delivery Order, while the remaining HUs in the original Outbound Delivery Order are kept in that Outbound Delivery Order. This scenario works in classic Advanced Shipping and Receiving loading as well as Immediate Loading.
  • Split remaining parts into new Outbound Delivery Order – the HUs in the Outbound Delivery Order that are to be delivered are selected, and the system splits the remaining items or HUs into a new Outbound Delivery Order. The selected HUs are kept in the original Outbound Delivery Order. For example, the HUs for an Outbound Delivery Order which are already fully picked, packed, and loaded to a specific transportation vehicle. However, the remaining parts of the Outbound Delivery Order will not be ready for picking, packing, or loading in time. The remaining parts are split to another Outbound Delivery Order and the loaded HUs are sent to the customer.

The setting for this functionality can be found in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Goods Issue Process > Outbound Delivery > Define Document Types for Outbound Delivery Process.  To enable splitting of Outbound Delivery Orders, Outbound Delivery Order Split must be chosen in the Outbound Split setting for the combination of document type and document category.

Harmonized SAP Global Trade Service integration

A final highlight I would like to mention in Advanced Shipping & Receiving is that the integration pattern for ASR and SAP Global Trade Services (SAP GTS) is now available for Extended Warehouse Management embedded in SAP S/4HANA. This integration pattern is harmonized with the integration pattern used in the decentralized setup for the Advanced Shipping and Receiving process (which is available since February 2025 with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release) and features enhance stability and reliability.

To enable the new integration pattern, users must ensure that the status type “DSC - LE Distribution Confirmation” is active in all relevant outbound delivery status profiles, in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Cross-Process Settings > Delivery - Warehouse Request > Status Management > Define Status Profiles.

SUITE INTEGRATION

Integration is a core pillar of our investments, by which we provide a deep and native integration with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud solution components, along with other SAP cloud solution offerings for seamless integration of comprehensive logistics end-to-end processes incl. production integration, customs processing and more. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 2025, FPS1 we deliver the following enhancements.

nJIT Supply-to-Customer (S2C)

Just-in-Time (JIT) processing is a common practice in manufacturing industries for efficient demand-driven production and logistics across supply chains. Just-In-Time Supply to Customer covers JIT processes from the perspective of a supplier. A JIT supply-to-customer(S2C) call is a request of the customer to deliver components to a ship-to location, specifying the requested quantity, date, time, and destination within the ship-to location for each item. The JIT S2C supply starts with the receipt of JIT calls from the customer. These JIT calls are directly relevant for shipping, assuming there is stock available from in-house production or external procurement. The shipping process starts with delivery creation and ends with posting goods issue. After goods issue, the settlement is done from creating the billing document until receiving the payment.

With the current release, we enable production planners, warehouse operators and shipping specialists to work with the S2C process for Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA in their storage locations. After the production and packing of the sequenced component groups, the system creates outbound deliveries using the Manage Customer JIT Calls by Component Groups app, including handling unit numbers from packing. These deliveries are then transferred to EWM. In EWM, the system creates an Outbound Delivery Order that contains the lowest-level HU details (to be used as pick-HUs) per delivery item. The HU warehouse tasks can be created manually or automatically to pick highest-level HUs and move them to the staging area. If HUs are already staged, the system automatically creates and confirms the corresponding pick tasks, reducing manual steps.

Quantity Correction Process in Advanced Returns Management (ARM)

There are cases when after material inspection is done for received customer returns, there rises a need to correct received quantities, for example when not the entire quantity was returned, or if upon inspection of the goods, some are beyond recovery and must be sent to salvage rather than storage.

With this release we enable logistical follow-up action “0023 - Goods Receipt Quantity Correction” to correct the goods receipt quantity after inspection so that discrepancies between the returned quantity and inspected quantity can be accurately reflected in the system. The outbound delivery is directly sent to Extended Warehouse Management with a corresponding document type and item type. Automatic warehouse task creation and confirmation to a staging zone, including an automatic goods issue posting is performed to enable the stock correction

To run the quantity correction process in Advanced Returns Management, users must activate business configuration set (BC set) Outbound Delivery Process for ARM Quantity Correction (/SCWM/DLV_OUTBOUND_ARM_QC). There is a proposed mapping for Logistics Execution (LE) document and item types to EWM document and item types in the BC set documentation.

Quality Inspections for Products in Outbound Delivery Orders

A final highlight worth spotlighting in the area of Suite Integration is the integration of quality management to outbound warehouse processes. With this integration, warehouse operators and quality inspectors can make sure that specific qualities of a product (for example, paperwork completion) are checked before the relevant Outbound Delivery Order leaves your warehouse. To this end, we introduced a new indicator on the EWM Outbound Delivery Order item – Relevant for Q-Inspection in Outbound.

With this indicator, the system checks the status of the quality inspection at goods issue posting in EWM and prevent successful posting in case quality inspection is not completed. The check prevents goods issue posting for EWM Outbound Delivery Orders with open inspection lots with origin:

  • 10 - Delivery to Customer with Sales Order
  • 11 - Delivery to Customer Without Sales Order
  • 12 - General Delivery

To use this feature, IOT 8 must be activated in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Cross-Process Settings > Quality Management > Basics and Integration > Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs.

CONTINUOUS MODERNIZATION & SIMPLIFICATION

Our commitment to modernization and continuous improvements not only enhances functionality but also provides users with a seamless and intuitive interaction, helping businesses drive efficiency, optimize processes, and deliver superior experiences to their stakeholders. To that end, I would like to highlight the following features from this latest release.

Use Stock Transfer Orders with Valuated Stock in Transit (SIT)

Starting with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 2025 FPS01 release, we enable the use of EWM-managed storage locations as receiving locations in the stock transfer process with valuated SIT. Valuated SIT can be used in both intra-company processes and processes that move stock between companies.

Quantify changes are not supported in the scenario, besides that an inbound delivery item with valuated SIT can be set to zero in a stock transfer order process, which cancels the delivery. Subsequently, the quantity of an inbound delivery item with valuated SIT cannot be changed to anything other than zero in a stock transfer order process.

The field EWMStockTransferOrderType is now also available in the OData API Warehouse Inbound Delivery - Read, Update (A2X) (API_WHSE_INB_DELIVERY_2). To enable this feature, the Prev. Qty Upd STO checkbox must be selected in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Goods Receipt Process > Inbound Delivery > Define Item Types for Inbound Delivery Process.

Specify Handling Unit Number for Component Consumption

In certain production environments, the Production Supply Areas (PSA) are managed by the Handling Units. Before this release, production warehouse workers had to consume the entire component group HU at the PSA before you can register the consumption in the system. However, there are cases when only select sub-HUs are required from the PSA. In order to keep the physical world and the system in sync, we now allow users to specify which HU the component is to be consumed from. This strengthened integration is supported for synchronous goods movement postings via the Production Order Confirmation API (API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV), which passes the handling unit (HU) number for component consumption with synchronous goods movements.

CLEAN CORE & EXTENSIBILITY

The Clean Core Extensibility strategy aims to provide a modern and powerful integration option for customers and partners. Alongside supporting this strategy, we continuously adapt solution content to modernize and simplify the user experience. By regularly updating and refining Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, we ensure we remain relevant and effective in meeting current needs.

In the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release we continue to deliver on this with the following highlights:

  • Update for the Warehouse Order and Task (A2X) API – We have enhanced the existing ODATA-service Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), with the new ‘Lock/Unlock Warehouse Order’ actions on the Warehouse Order. This was done to enable efficient and lean integration of warehouse automation (e.g. robotics).
  • Enhancement of Stock List in the Warehouse Task Apps – We enabled warehouse operators to add their own fields to the stock list at warehouse task creation for warehouse request. For this, they can use the Business Add-In (BAdI) Enhancement for Stock List for Warehouse Task Creation (/SCWM/EX_CORE_CR_STOCK_LIST) to add additional fields to the display of stock at warehouse task creation for warehouse requests in the following apps:
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Inbound Delivery
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Outbound Delivery Order
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Posting Change
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Stock Transfer

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above-mentioned topics as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release (such as RF improvement to allow RF receiving of mixed HUs with Serial Numbers, deletion of Inbound Deliveries in the STO scenario, and more) I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2025, Private Edition, FPS01” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: Develop your knowledge of SAP Cloud HANA Knowledge Graph
entry.content:
A knowledge graph is an essential technology in the development of intelligent applications. Knowledge graphs have multiple use cases including the grounding of AI so that results are reliable and verifiable, and also for use in search engines to bring back accurate and helpful results. 

SAP provides the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph as a component of SAP HANA Cloud so customers can get started right away with no extra license fees to consider.

Join us for a 1 hour Live Session Getting Started with SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph.  Our guest speaker is Shabana Samsudheen, who is the SAP Product Manager for SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph. She will provide a technical overview and some real uses cases, and of course, answer your questions.

Click below to register. (subscription to SAP Learning Hub required)

Getting Started with the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph

entry.title: “Dynamic Field Manipulation in RAP Action Popup – A Step-by-Step Guide”
entry.content:
Introduction-

In SAP RAP, actions play a crucial role in triggering business logic from the UI. One powerful feature is the ability to open a parameter dialog (popup) when an action is executed. 
In this blog, we will explore how to dynamically control fields inside a RAP action popup, including implementing dropdown value help and runtime field manipulation. This step-by-step guide demonstrates how to design, implement, and test a parameterized RAP action that enhances user interaction in SAP Fiori Elements.

Let's create the tables-

Header table-

@EndUserText.label : 'Contact Table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zbrt_t_contact { 

key client         : abap.clnt not null; 
key contact_id     : sysuuid_x16 not null; 
@EndUserText.label : 'First Name' 
first_name         : abap.char(30) not null; 
@EndUserText.label : 'Middle Name' 
middle_name        : abap.char(30); 
@EndUserText.label : 'Last Name' 
last_name          : abap.char(30); 
gender             : zbrt_de_gender; 
@EndUserText.label : 'Date of Birth' 
dob                : abap.dats; 
@EndUserText.label : 'Age' 
age                : abap.numc(3); 
@EndUserText.label : 'Telephone' 
telephone          : abap.numc(10); 
@EndUserText.label : 'Email' 
email              : abap.char(80); 
active             : abap_boolean; 
created_by         : abp_creation_user; 
created_at         : abp_creation_tstmpl; 
last_changed_by    : abp_lastchange_user; 
last_changed_at    : abp_lastchange_tstmpl; 
}

Item table with foreign key relationship-

@EndUserText.label : 'Contact address table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zbrt_t_contaddr { 

key client      : abap.clnt not null; 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false 
key contact_id  : sysuuid_x16 not null 
with foreign key [1..*,1] zbrt_t_contact 
where client = zbrt_t_contaddr.client 
and contact_id = zbrt_t_contaddr.contact_id; 
key address_id  : sysuuid_x16 not null; 
key address_sr  : abap.numc(2) not null; 
addr1           : abap.char(30); 
addr2           : abap.char(30); 
city            : abap.char(20); 
state           : abap.char(30); 
pincode         : abap.numc(6); 
created_by      : abp_creation_user; 
created_at      : abp_creation_tstmpl; 
last_changed_by : abp_lastchange_user; 
last_changed_at : abp_lastchange_tstmpl; 
}

Then create the interface view along with association and composition--

Header interface view--

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Contact View'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity Zi_Brt_Contact
as select from zbrt_t_contact
composition[0..*] of ZI_BRT_CONTADDR as _Address
association[1..*] to ZI_BRT_GENDER as _Gender
on $projection.Gender = _Gender.GenderCode
{
key contact_id as ContactId,
first_name as FirstName,
middle_name as MiddleName,
last_name as LastName,
gender as Gender,
dob as Dob,
age as Age,
telephone as Telephone,
email as Email,
active as Active,
created_by as CreatedBy,
created_at as CreatedAt, 
last_changed_by as LastChangedBy, 
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt, 
_Gender, 
_Address 
}

Item interface view--

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Address view'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view entity ZI_BRT_CONTADDR
as select from zbrt_t_contaddr
association to parent Zi_Brt_Contact as _Contact
on $projection.ContactId = _Contact.ContactId
{
key contact_id as ContactId,
key address_id as AddressId,
key address_sr as AddressSr,
addr1 as Addr1,
addr2 as Addr2,
city as City,
state as State,
pincode as Pincode,
created_by as CreatedBy,
created_at as CreatedAt,
last_changed_by as LastChangedBy,
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt,
_Contact 
}

Then create the projection view for both header and item--

Header projection view--

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection view for contact'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define root view entity zc_brt_contact
provider contract transactional_query
as projection on Zi_Brt_Contact
{
key ContactId,
FirstName,
MiddleName,
LastName,
// @ObjectModel.text.association: '_Gender'
Gender,
Dob,
Age,
Telephone,
Email,
Active,
CreatedBy,
CreatedAt,
LastChangedBy,
LastChangedAt,
/* Associations */
_Gender,
_Address : redirected to composition child zc_brt_contaddr
}

Item projection view--

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Address projection view'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view entity zc_brt_contaddr
as projection on ZI_BRT_CONTADDR
{
key ContactId,
key AddressId,
key AddressSr,
Addr1,
Addr2,
City,
State,
Pincode,
CreatedBy,
CreatedAt,
LastChangedBy,
LastChangedAt,
/* Associations */
_Contact : redirected to parent zc_brt_contact
}

Then create Behavior definition on interface view--

unmanaged implementation in class zbrt_cl_contact_saver unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for Zi_Brt_Contact alias u_contact
implementation in class zbrt_cl_bp_contact_u unique
draft table zbrt_t_contact_d
late numbering
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master ( instance )
//etag master <field_name>
{
create;
update ( features : instance );
delete ( features : instance );
field ( readonly ) ContactId, Age;

association _Address { create (features : instance); with draft; }

determination calculate_age on modify { field Dob; }
action ( features : instance ) setstatus result[1] $self;
action change_field_value parameter ZI_BRT_STATUS_PARAM result[1] $self;

draft action Edit;
draft action Activate optimized;
draft action Resume;
draft action Discard;
draft determine action Prepare; 
mapping for zbrt_t_contact control zbrt_s_contact
{
    ContactId     = contact_id;
    FirstName     = first_name;
    MiddleName    = middle_name;
    LastName      = last_name;
    Gender        = gender;
    Dob           = dob;
    Age           = age;
    Telephone     = telephone;
    Email         = email;
    Active        = active;
    CreatedBy     = created_by;
    CreatedAt     = created_at;
    LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
    LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
  }
}
define behavior for ZI_BRT_CONTADDR alias u_contadd
implementation in class zbrt_cl_bp_contadd_u unique
draft table zbrt_t_contadd_d
late numbering
lock dependent by _Contact
authorization dependent by _Contact
{
update (features : instance);
delete (features : instance);
field ( readonly ) ContactId, AddressId, AddressSr;
association _Contact{with draft;}
mapping for zbrt_t_contaddr control zbrt_s_contaddr
{
    ContactId     = contact_id;
    AddressId     = address_id;
    AddressSr     = address_sr;
    Addr1         = addr1;
    Addr2         = addr2;
    City          = city;
    State         = state;
    Pincode       = pincode;
    CreatedBy     = created_by;
    CreatedAt     = created_at;
    LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
    LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
  }
}

Behavior definition on projection view--

projection;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;

define behavior for zc_brt_contact //alias u_contact
use etag
{
use create;
use update;
use delete;
use action setstatus;
use action change_field_value;

use action Edit;
use action Activate;
use action Resume;
use action Discard;
use action Prepare;

use association _Address { create; with draft; }
}
define behavior for zc_brt_contaddr //alias u_contaddr
{
use update;
use delete;
use association _Contact { with draft; }
}

Service definition--

Service binding--

Actions buttons in UI-

This is the action button declared in the behavior definition-->

This method setstatus is a RAP Action implementation. 
It toggles the status (ACTIVE ↔ INACTIVE) of a Contact entity.

%tky- 

  • Technical key 
  • Required to update the same instance that was read 
  • Mandatory in MODIFY ENTITIES

UPDATE FIELDS ( active )-->

  • Only updates the active field 
  • Prevents accidental changes to other fields

After declaring action name in the behavior definition, we need to declare it in the metadata extension also--

In above we can see the action button name Change Status in the UI 

Based on the Active status it is going to change. 

Let's see--

Now the field abc -> Active status is Yes -> click on the Change Status button -> now it will become No. 

IMP - Now here we have another action button which is a parameterized action button which is declared using abstract entity.

Change-Field-Value.

For that we have already created one Abstract entity --

@EndUserText.label: 'Status parameter'
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define abstract entity zi_brt_status_param
{
 .defaultValue  : 'X' 
 @EndUserText.label: 'Active'
 active            : abap_boolean;
 .defaultValue: #( 'ELEMENT_OF_REFERENCE_ENTITY : Telephone' )
 @EndUserText.label: 'Telephone'
 telephone         : abap.numc(10);
}

Then declared the same action name in the metadata also- 

See the logic--

METHOD change_field_value.

DATA(ls_param) = VALUE #( keys[ 1 ]-%param ).

READ ENTITIES OF zi_brt_contact IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY u_contact
ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
RESULT DATA(lt_contact).

MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_brt_contact IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY u_contact
UPDATE FIELDS ( active telephone )
WITH VALUE #(
   FOR lwa_contact IN lt_contact (
   %tky = lwa_contact-%tky
   %data-active = ls_param-active
   %data-telephone = ls_param-telephone
  ) ).

result = VALUE #( FOR lwa_contact IN lt_contact (
                  %key = lwa_contact-%key
                  %param = CORRESPONDING #( lwa_contact )
               ) ). 
ENDMETHOD.

This is an action implementation method inside the Behavior Implementation Class. 

It updates: 

  • active
  • telephone

based on the values entered in the RAP action popup parameter dialog. 

Fetch Action Parameters from Popup

DATA(ls_param) = VALUE #( keys[ 1 ]-%param ).
  • keys → contains selected instances + action parameters 
  • %param → holds popup field values entered by user 
  • keys[ 1 ] → since action usually triggered on selected row(s) 

Meaning: 

We are extracting the parameter values entered in the popup into structure ls_param. 

If popup had: 

  • Active = ‘X’ 
  • Telephone = ‘9876543210’ 

Those values now sit in ls_param. 

Read Current Entity Data--

READ ENTITIES OF zi_brt_contact IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY u_contact
  ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
  RESULT DATA(lt_contact).
  • Reading selected records from BO 
  • IN LOCAL MODE → no DB commit, transactional buffer 
  • WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys ) → read only selected instances 
  • Result stored in lt_contact 

Why required? 

Because: 

  • We need technical key %tky 
  • We need current data before updating 
  • EML update requires %tky 

Update Fields Using MODIFY ENTITIES-

MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_brt_contact IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY u_contact
  UPDATE FIELDS ( active telephone )
  WITH VALUE #(
    FOR lwa_contact IN lt_contact (
      %tky = lwa_contact-%tky
      %data-active = ls_param-active
      %data-telephone = ls_param-telephone
    ) 
  ).

We are updating: 

  • active 
  • telephone

for each selected instance. 

Important Points: 

✔ %tky → technical key required to identify instance 
✔ %data-fieldname → actual field update 
✔ UPDATE FIELDS → performance optimized (only those 2 fields updated) 

Note - Even though popup opened per selected row, internally RAP still processes in mass-enabled mode. 

That’s why:

FOR lwa_contact IN lt_contact

This allows:

  • Single selection 
  • Multiple selection 
  • Bulk update

Return Result to UI

result = VALUE #(
  FOR lwa_contact IN lt_contact (
    %key = lwa_contact-%key
    %param = CORRESPONDING #( lwa_contact )
  )
).

After update: 

  • Returning updated instance(s) 
  • %key → identifies updated instance 
  • %param → sends data back to UI

    User clicks Action 
    ↓ 
    Popup opens 
    ↓ 
    User enters Active + Telephone 
    ↓ 
    %param receives values 
    ↓ 
    READ ENTITIES → fetch selected records 
    ↓ 
    MODIFY ENTITIES → update selected instances 
    ↓ 
    Return result → UI refresh

Let's see how it works--

Now I have selected one record -> Bikash -> which telephone Num is there, and Active status is Yes -> click on Change-field-value ->

-> we can see the pop-up where we can change the Active status drop down and Telephone number as well -> let’s do the changes and see ->

In the above, we can see the changes. 

Conclusion--

In this blog, we explored how to implement dynamic field manipulation inside a RAP action popup using a parameterized action. We walked through extracting popup parameters using %param, reading selected instances via READ ENTITIES, and performing controlled updates using MODIFY ENTITIES in local mode. Finally, we ensured proper UI synchronization by returning the updated result set back to the Fiori Elements application. 

Thanks--

entry.title: Re: Consignment process and Sales of Non-Stock Item with...
entry.content:
1. Two‑Step Process (most common in Public Cloud)
Step 1: Create the BDN sales order to procure the product from the external supplier.
Step 2: Once the product is received, transfer it into customer consignment stock using a consignment fill‑up order (transaction in Public Cloud scope item BD9).
Step 3: Customer consumes stock → consignment issue → billing triggered.

This way, procurement is handled by BDN, and consignment logic is handled by BD9, but they are linked operationally by the material flow.

2. Alternative: Direct Consignment Procurement
Instead of BDN, procure the product directly into consignment stock for the customer. This requires customizing the procurement type so that the inbound delivery posts directly into consignment stock.

Limitation: Not always available in Public Cloud standard scope, so you may need to check if your tenant has the relevant configuration.

3. Custom Bridging Logic (if business insists on one document)
Use a custom CDS view or RAP logic to link the BDN order line to the consignment fill‑up order automatically. The system still creates two documents, but the user experience is simplified (they see one “flow”). This is the lightest custom approach, since you’re not changing core processes, just automating the link.

entry.title: Joule Capabilities for Developers ABAP
entry.content:
Introduction :

Joule is SAP’s AI-powered copilot designed to assist developers and business users across SAP applications. For developers, Joule offers:

  • Context-aware code suggestions.
  • Real-time explanations of ABAP objects.
  • Automation of repetitive tasks.
  • Support for full-stack ABAP Cloud development.

Joule Capabilities for Developers

Feature

Description

Code Prediction

Suggests next lines of ABAP code contextually

Object Explanation

Provides real-time insights into ABAP objects

Scenario Generation

Creates full-stack development templates

Legacy Code Support

Helps interpret and refactor old codebases

Productivity Boost

Reduces development and testing time by ~20–25%

Prerequisites

Before starting the setup, ensure the following components are available:

Component

Purpose

SAP BTP Subaccount

Hosting and managing services

Cloud Foundry Environment

Runtime for deploying applications

SAP Cloud Identity Services

Authentication and SSO

SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition)

UI integration and launchpad

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

Development environment

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

ERP system with Joule capabilities

 License:

To leverage Joule’s capabilities, you need to purchase an additional license:

  1. Customers: 8019124 - Joule for developers limited promotion, ABAP AI
  2. Partners (TDD Subscription): 8019541 - Cloud TDD, Joule for developers ABAP AI

For more details, please check SAP Note 3571857

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition the subscription needs to be requested via SAP for Me

Product availability:

  1. SAP BTP ABAP Environment as of release 2502
  2. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as of release 2502
  3. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition as of release 2025.

Setup:

Embedded Joule: It is used by Business users. Below URL for joule setup.

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup ... - SAP Community

Joule for Developers

It is used by Developers in ADT (Eclipse).

Set up in  Public cloud environment : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-for-developers-with-sap-s-4hana-public-cloud-edition-setup-guide/ba-p/14209989

Set up in BTP environment:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-for-developers-with-sap-btp-abap-environment-setup-guide/ba-p/14207462

Joule for Developers ABAP : Helps developers in below use cases

  • Predictive Code Completion: Suggests next lines of code based on context.
  • Code Explanation: Understand legacy or complex ABAP and CDS code
  •  OData consumption helpers: Generate client proxy calls
  •  RAP business objects generation
  •  Automated unit test generation for ABAP

Predictive code completion

In RAP development, the system interprets the developer’s intent and recommends appropriate code, as demonstrated in the screenshot below.

Alternatively, the system can generate code from natural‑language prompts. To do this, position the cursor inside the method and choose Quick Help.

Click Run button after entering the prompt. Look at code generated in below screen shot.

Code explanation:

To understand the functionality of any class or CDS view, open the class and type “/”, then select the Explain option as mentioned below screen shot.

Loot at the generated summary for selected class.

OData consumption:

The Service Consumption Model in ABAP (created in ABAP Development Tools, ADT/Eclipse) generates a typed OData client proxy from an OData metadata (EDMX/service URL). You then use that proxy to call an external OData service in a safe, OO way—no manual HTTP/JSON handling needed.

Use a Service Consumption Model if you need to consume (call) an external OData service from ABAP—for example:

  • Reading products/orders from a partner system (OData V2 or V4).
  • Calling SAP S/4HANA Cloud APIs (which are exposed as OData).
  • Integrating public demo services (e.g., Northwind) for POCs.

Choose mode as OData and click on finish.

Enter the name of the class and upload the metadata file.

Loot at generated one and sample code for entity set access.

Create one global class to consume the model

Right click on model and choose consume option from Joule.

Copy and paste source code from Joule.

RAP business objects generation:

In the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), a Business Object (BO) represents the data model, behaviour, and business logic of an application. A RAP BO is generated using a set of design‑time artifacts.

A complete RAP BO typically includes:

  1. Data Model (via CDS Views)
  2. Behaviour Definition (BDEF)
  3. Behaviour Implementation (ABAP classes)
  4. Service Definition
  5. Service Binding

Right click on the package and choose Generate ABAP Repository Objects as mentioned below screen shot.

Choose OData UI Service from Scratch

Look at Joule icon in the left side of the screen.

Enter the prompt as per our requirement.

Click on Accept

Look at generated table and adjust or add any fields if required.

Look at generated artifacts.

Click on Next button and Observe the system generated Artifacts.

Activate the Service and test the service.

Automated unit test generation for ABAP:

Joule can generate ABAP Unit tests for public, protected, and private methods of global classes, and for public methods of local classes in global class pools.

CDS test classes (CDS unit tests) in addition to ABAP class tests.

Right click on class and choose Joule with Unit option i.e. / Unit.

Select Generate test for selected methods.

Tick check boxes methods which are required.

Look at generated test class by the Joule.

 

For testing, Right click on class and choose ABAP Unit test.

Look at system generate results as mentioned in screen.
