entry.title: What’s New in the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 release
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 release brings a new level of intelligence to everyday business work by expanding how AI supports users across various LoBs. At the core of this update is Joule and SAP’s agentic AI framework, where specialized agents and conversational capabilities work together to guide users through tasks, surface the right information, and provide recommendations in the moment of need. Instead of navigating complex transactions or piecing together data from multiple systems, employees can rely on natural‑language interactions to move through their processes faster and with greater confidence. This release also delivers embedded AI features that automate checks, streamline validations, and support operational decisions with real‑time insights. Altogether, these innovations help organizations reduce manual effort, strengthen transparency, and keep their operations running more smoothly, while allowing users to focus on the work that truly drives business outcomes.

Agentic use cases

Finance:

Mastering Cash Flow with the Cash Management Agent*

The Cash Management Agent supports finance teams by automating tasks that usually drain time, checking bank statement completeness, validating opening balances, and preparing cash‑flow forecasts. Many organizations struggle with fragmented financial data and delayed insights, which makes it difficult to spot potential cash shortages or opportunities to use surpluses effectively. By relying on Joule and Joule Agents to run these daily checks, calculations, and summaries, teams can shift from manual reconciliation to confident, data‑driven decisions. This automation improves accuracy and frees cash managers to focus on strategic liquidity planning instead of routine monitoring. In the end, it strengthens daily cash‑position visibility and helps organizations manage their cash resources more efficiently.

Learn more by accessing the SAP Help Portal.

* This AI offering is currently in Beta and available at no cost. Pricing will be communicated once the AI offering becomes generally available. A separate subscription is required for use or testing. Learn more.

 

R&D:

Streamlined engineering changes with the change record management*

Engineering teams often lose momentum when a simple product or process change turns into a maze of documentation, reviews, and approvals. Coordinators and engineers must manually create change records and shepherd them through the process, which can delay decisions and slow time‑to‑market. The Change Record Management Agent uses SAP Joule to assist with creating complete and consistent change records and orchestrating the necessary workflow steps. By reducing manual effort and guiding teams through the process, it helps engineering organizations keep changes moving without administrative bottlenecks. This leads to a faster, more efficient change‑management cycle and ultimately supports quicker delivery of updates and innovations.


 Use our SAP Help page to learn more.

 

Joule capabilities

Please note that certain Joule features described in this blog may not yet be available in your system and are planned for release as part of the upcoming Joule release cycle scheduled for March/April 2026.

Finance:

Joule for invoicing specialists to enable more-seamless navigation and transactional actions within billing documents

Invoicing specialists often spend far too much time clicking through multiple screens just to find the right convergent invoicing app or complete a billing‑related task. When workloads are high, even simple actions, like navigating to a billing document or making a small update, can slow the process and impact how quickly customers receive clear and accurate invoices. With expanded Joule capabilities, specialists can move directly to the right SAP Fiori apps and perform transactional actions through natural‑language prompts, without hunting for the correct tile or transaction. This conversational support reduces manual effort and helps teams resolve billing tasks more efficiently, leading to quicker processing and a better customer experience. Over time, this can even help reduce days sales outstanding by keeping billing workflows running smoothly and consistently.

AI-assisted accrual object creation

Preparing manual accruals often requires finance teams to gather fragmented information, apply judgment, and cross‑check historical patterns, all of which can be time‑consuming and inconsistent across entities. When deadlines are tight, variations in how preparers estimate amounts or select accounts can increase the risk of missed accruals or adjustments later in the audit process. With AI‑assisted accrual creation in Joule, users can describe their scenario in natural language and instantly receive a proposed accrual object, including category, amount, life term, and relevant GL accounts. They can refine any attribute, and Joule adapts its suggestions in real time based on the edits, gradually improving proposal quality through continued use. This guided, conversational approach streamlines preparation, increases consistency, and helps finance teams create more accurate accruals with far less effort.

 

 

Sales:

Staying ahead with AI-assisted detecting of expiring prices using Joule 

Pricing specialists often spend valuable time hunting through condition records to find prices that are about to expire, a task that becomes even more demanding when large volumes of data and tight sales timelines are involved. Missed expirations can lead to outdated pricing in customer quotes, last‑minute firefighting, and unnecessary manual corrections. With Joule detecting expiring prices, displaying relevant condition records, and even supporting validity extensions and mass changes, specialists can handle follow‑up activities with far less effort. This reduces repetitive work, minimizes human error, and keeps pricing information accurate and up to date across the system. Ultimately, it enables teams to manage pricing proactively, saving both time and operational cost.

 

 

Use our SAP Help page to learn more.

 

AI-assisted fetching and listing of open sales documents

Internal sales reps often need to scan through multiple criteria in VA05 just to find the right open sales orders, which can slow down workflows when customers are waiting for updates. Searching by document type, dates, materials, or organizational data requires navigating several fields and filters, making the process repetitive and time‑consuming. With AI‑assisted retrieval in Joule, reps can simply ask for open sales documents, and the system instantly displays orders filtered by attributes such as customer, material, creation date, or sales area. This conversational approach reduces the manual effort needed to locate the right information and helps reps respond faster and more accurately. The result is a more efficient sales process and smoother daily operations for the team.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

AI-assisted Sales Quotation Changes using Joule 

Updating sales quotations can be surprisingly time‑consuming, especially when internal sales reps need to adjust quantities, refine pricing, change delivery dates, or capture customer‑specific references across multiple line items. These routine updates often involve navigating several fields in VA22, increasing the chance of small mistakes that can slow down the sales cycle. With AI‑assisted quotation changes in Joule, reps can make these adjustments through simple natural‑language requests, whether they need to update a quantity, modify a price via condition type, set delivery dates, or record reasons for rejection. This conversational flow speeds up the process and reduces manual effort, helping teams respond to customer requests more quickly and accurately. The result is a smoother quotation‑management experience that keeps sales moving with less friction.

 

Service:

Searching and navigating to service contracts with Joule

Service agents often spend more time searching for the right service contract than actually solving the customer’s issue, especially when navigating by status, dates, or complex contract structures. These delays can disrupt workflows and make it harder to move quickly from one task to the next. With Joule assisting through natural‑language prompts, agents can instantly search for service contracts, open details, complete releases, or jump directly to the relevant SAP Fiori functionality. This reduces unnecessary clicks and helps users focus on completing service‑related tasks instead of trying to find them. As a result, teams can deliver faster responses and manage service contracts with more confidence and less effort.

 

 

Joule for transferring in-house service objects

Transferring an in‑house service object between plants or storage locations often requires navigating several steps in the system, from creating the outbound delivery to recording the inbound receipt. These tasks can slow down technicians and coordinators who simply want to keep service operations moving. With Joule guiding the process through conversational prompts, users can initiate the transfer, add the target plant and storage location, and create the necessary deliveries without searching for the right transaction. This reduces friction in daily logistics and helps teams complete handovers more reliably. The conversational flow not only speeds up execution but also makes the transfer process easier for occasional users who don’t perform it every day.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

 

Supply Chain:

Enabling Joule to drive operational efficiency in outbound processes in SAP Extended Warehouse Management

Warehouse teams often juggle multiple screens just to track outbound deliveries, check warehouse tasks, or confirm the status of orders—slowing down operations in environments where every minute counts. As outbound volumes grow and customer expectations tighten, the effort required to manually search, summarize, and update records can become a real bottleneck. With Joule integrated into SAP EWM, users can search for outbound delivery orders, review warehouse tasks, or even create and confirm warehouse orders simply by asking in natural language. This gives frontline teams instant insights and reduces the time spent navigating complex menus or transactions. The result is faster decision‑making, smoother outbound processing, and a more intuitive experience that helps warehouses keep goods moving efficiently.

 To learn more, access our SAP Help Portal.

 

R&D:

Project assistant capabilities for the Joule copilot: read cost and revenue details of project objects

Project controllers and R&D teams often spend a lot of time gathering cost and revenue details from multiple reports, especially when they need to compare actuals, planned values, commitments, or fiscal‑year breakdowns. Navigating through different project objects, whether a project, WBS element, network, or network activity, can slow decision‑making and make routine checks feel heavier than they need to be. With Joule acting as a project assistant, users can simply ask for the financial picture of any project object and instantly receive details such as actual cost, planned cost, commitments, or revenue, including breakdowns by fiscal year, cost component, or value category. This conversational access removes the need to open complex reports or consolidate information manually, giving teams a clearer view of project performance in seconds. The result is faster insights, easier financial monitoring, and a more intuitive way for project stakeholders to stay on top of their work.

 Consult our SAP Help Page for more information.

 

Streamlining batch master insights and navigation with Joule

Reviewing batch master data often requires users to navigate across multiple fields and apps to check details such as material, plant, batch status, manufacturing date, shelf-life expiry date, or supplier, which can slow decision-making and increase the risk of missed insights under tight timelines. With Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, users can describe their requirement in natural language and instantly retrieve relevant batch master information, then navigate directly to the Manage Batches object page for deeper analysis, updates, or next actions. This conversational approach streamlines access to batch insights, enhances user experience, and improves operational efficiency through faster, more intuitive interaction.

 

Embedded AI

Finance:

AI-assisted fixed asset key figures explanation

Fixed asset reporting often raises questions that require deep accounting knowledge, especially when values are influenced by midyear acquisitions, depreciation keys, or complex calculation rules. In many organizations, answering these questions means digging through configuration tables or asking specialists to explain how a specific number was derived. With AI‑assisted explanations in Joule, users can simply request a breakdown of any fixed asset key figure and immediately see where the values come from and how they were calculated. This natural‑language guidance helps preparers and reviewers understand depreciation logic and valuation outcomes without manual analysis, reducing the risk of errors or compliance issues. As a result, teams can resolve fixed‑asset questions more quickly and consistently, with far less effort during period close or audits.

 

 

AI-assisted settlement rule proposal for asset capitalization  

Creating settlement rules for investment measures can be a painstaking task, especially when projects or internal orders need to settle to fixed assets and no automatic guidance is available. Finance teams often spend time determining the right receivers and percentages, increasing the risk of inconsistencies across measures or late adjustments when assets are capitalized. With AI‑assisted settlement rule proposals in Joule, users receive an initial suggestion for full settlement to fixed assets and can easily refine the proposal by adjusting percentages or selecting different receivers. This conversational support reduces manual effort and helps ensure more accurate asset class determination for final capitalization. The result is a smoother, more reliable settlement process that supports finance teams without adding complexity.

 

 

Supply Chain:

AI-assisted goods receipt analysis for bordero documents 

Goods receipt processing for bordero documents can be slow and error‑prone, especially when teams need to manually review carrier paperwork, create corresponding freight orders, and verify that all information matches what’s in the system. These manual checks often lead to delays at the gate, longer truck turnaround times, and unnecessary waiting for logistics partners. With SAP Document AI analyzing bordero documents, the system can automatically extract key details, propose a freight order, and highlight discrepancies between the uploaded document and existing freight data. This reduces the time spent on validations, improves accuracy, and helps teams process inbound goods more efficiently. The smoother flow not only enhances operational performance but can also reduce yard congestion by keeping vehicles moving.

 

Summary

As you look across the innovations in this release, a clear pattern emerges: AI is no longer something users “go to”, it’s something that quietly supports them throughout their day. Whether it’s a cash manager starting the morning with a clear liquidity picture, a service agent navigating directly to the right contract, or a warehouse coordinator checking outbound tasks with a simple question, Joule and embedded AI make these moments feel more connected and intuitive. These capabilities reduce the friction of everyday work, giving teams more room to focus on decisions, collaboration, and the tasks that genuinely move the business forward. And while this release delivers meaningful improvements on its own, it also lays the foundation for even more intelligent assistance, with agents and AI features becoming part of the natural rhythm of how people use SAP Cloud ERP. It’s a step toward a future where systems feel less like tools and more like partners, helping organizations work smarter, not harder. 

Thank you for taking the time to explore the latest updates of AI‑driven capabilities in SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition with us. If you’d like to stay informed about upcoming features, deep dives, and enablement content, make sure to follow updates tagged with PSCC_Enablement in the SAP Community. You’re also welcome to connect with me (@Yannick_PTT) both in the community and on LinkedIn for more insights and continuous updates.

For a deeper look into the intelligent capabilities delivered in this release, you can explore the full documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

More information

  • What’s New Viewer
  • From Innovation to Impact: SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 Release (SAP News Center)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Community
  • Help Portal SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • What's New for Joule Capabilities
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Business Suite Product Expert Training H1 2026

We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Advanced Intercompany Sales Process - SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Advanced Intercompany Sales

This  Blog is divided into two parts. This part has the details of the configuration and master data Setup required for the Advanced Intercompany Sales Process. The Second Part will have the details of the process flow. 

Intercompany Sales 

Intercompany Sales Scenarios is used when a customer's fulfilment request is fulfilled from a plant that belongs to a different company code within the corporate group. This was introduced in R3 to have the slim design with minimal transactional documents.

Classic Intercompany Sales

Process Flow:

  1. A Sales Order will be created for a customer's Purchase Order.
  2. The Plant will be changed in the sales order to the Plant (Delivering Plant) that belong to a different company code.
  3. An Outbound Delivery will be created with reference to Sales order
  4. Goods issue will be posted from the Delivering Plant.
  5. Physical Goods will be shipped from delivering plant to customer.
  6. Customer Invoice will be generated to customer.
  7. An intercompany invoice will be generated for Delivering Plant.

This Process flow had following drawbacks.

  1. Though the Goods were procured from a different legal entity (Plant) , there were no transactional documents generated for Purchase of this.
  2. No Stock was valuated while in Transit.
  3. This process was not aligned with IFRS15 (International Financial Reporting Standards)

Advanced Intercompany Sales

To overcome the drawbacks of the intercompany process, SAP has introduced the Advanced intercompany process. The Advanced intercompany process is a broadened version of the intercompany process.

Process Flow:

  1. A Sales Order will be created for a customer's Purchase Order.
  2. The Plant will be changed in the sales order to the Plant (Delivering Plant) that belong to a different company code.
  1. A Purchase Order will be created from the Sales Order. (Intercompany PO)
  2. A Sales Order will be created from the Purchase Order.  (Intercompany SO)
  3. An Outbound Delivery will be created with reference to the Customer Sales Order.
  4. Goods Issue will be posted with reference to the Outbound Delivery and following documents will be generated. This GI will move the Stock from delivering plant to Stock in Transit Plant.
    1. GI from Delivering Plant to Stock In Transit (SIT) Plant
    2. GR in the SIT plant
    3. GI from SIT Plant
  1. After the Goods movements from the Outbound Delivery, the following documents will be generated:
    1. An intercompany customer's invoice will be generated for the intercompany Sales Order
    2. A Supplier invoice will be created for the intercompany PO (invoice receipt for the intercompany PO)
    3. An invoice will be generated for the Customer Sales Order.

Prerequisites for Advanced Intercompany Sales

Value Chain Monitoring: In the above process, the subsequent documents like intercompany PO, Intercompany SO, Goods Movement postings, and invoice Postings, will be posted automatically. This is being achieved with the help of the technical Job "SAP_VCM_STEP_EXEC_ICSA". This needs to be activated.

Path: SPRO > IMG > ABAP Platform>Application Server>System Administration> Activation of Scope dependent Back Ground Job Definitions.

SAP Note: 3283316

Example Scenario

A Customer from 1010 Sales Organization places an order. The Delivery will be done from Plant 1710, which is part of company code 1710. Company code 1710 will raise an intercompany invoice to company code 1010.

 

Enterprise Component

Selling Company

Delivering Company

Company Code

1010

1710

Sales Org

1010

1710

Distribution Chain

1010-10

1710-10

Sales Area

1010-10-00

1710-10-00

Plant

1010

1710

Purchase Org

1010

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Customer

71091

 

Material

TG12

TG12

 

System Setup – Advanced Intercompany Sales

For Ease of Understanding the following configuration will be explained in the sequence of the process flow. To differentiate the steps between master data and the configuration, {M} &{C} will be used.

Master Data Setup {M} and the configuration Setup {C}.

Customer Master {M} 

Customer Master should exist in the selling company's sales org and company code. This is the customer master of End Customer (Consumer).

Material Master {M}

Material Master should be available and extended to both the selling and delivering companies' sales organizations and plants.

Legal Entity

Selling Company (1010)

Delivering Company (1710)

Sales Org

 

 

 

 

Plant

 

 

 

 

Assignment of Delivering Plant in Selling Company's Distribution chain {C}

To enable the change of Plant in the shipping Tab of the Customer Sales Order, the delivering plant should be assigned to the Selling Company's Distribution Chain.

Path: SPRO > IMG > Enterprise Structure>Assignment>Sales and Distribution> Assign Sales Org - Distribution Channel-Plant

Assignment of the Transit Plant{C}

The Change of the Delivering plant will enable the new field "Transit Plant" in the Sales Order line item's Shipping tab. The Plant available in this field is used for material valuation while the stock is in transit. The Material Master should be assigned to this plant. This can be a virtual plant or a standard plant.

Path: SPRO > IMG > Enterprise Structure>Assignment>Logistics General> Assign Transit Plant to Company Code (Advanced Inter Company)

Converting the Delivering Plant as internal Supplier

The Change of the shipping point in the customer sales order will lead to the creation of the intercompany Purchase Order and intercompany Sales Order.

For creating the Purchase Order from the Customer Sales Order, the delivering plant should be created as a vendor. Delivering Plant (1710) should be created as a vendor in the Selling Company Code's Purchase Org (1010).

A Business Partner representing the Supplying plant is created in the Delivering Plant's Company code (1010). The same Business Partner will be extended as a supplier (FLVN01) in the Selling Company's Company Code and Purchase Org

The plant code is assigned to this Business Partner. This is the link between the BP and Plant code.

This link will be used in determining the supplier to create the intercompany PO when a plant in the sales order line item belongs to a different company code.

Assign the Sales Area to the Plant {C}

The Delivering Plant should be assigned to the Sales Area. The Sales Area assigned in this step will be used to generate the intercompany invoice from the delivering plant to the Selling Plant's company code with the internal Customer.

SPRO > IMG > Sales and Distribution>Billing >Intercompany Billing >Assign Organizational Units By Plant >Assign Organizational Units to the Plants

Create the Purchase Info Records {M}

The PIR is used to hold the supplier and material-specific details, which will be used at the time of PO creation. While creating the intercompany PO, the system uses the PIR details and populates the necessary fields in the PO. If the price is maintained in the PIR, then these prices will be used to calculate the transfer price.

Transaction Code: MEK1

Converting the Transit Plant / Selling Plant as an Internal Customer

The Intercompany Purchase Order creation will lead to the creation of the Intercompany Sales Order Creation.

For an intercompany sales order, an internal customer is required. Hence, the selling company's Plant will be created as an internal customer in the Delivering Company's Company code.

The Selling Plant is linked to this Business Partner. This is the link between Plant and BP.

Value Chain Category Assignment to Sales{C}

This is the Crucial configuration in Advanced Intercompany Sale Process".

Assign the Sales Document type to the Value Chain Category

To allow the Intercompany Sales order to be created automatically using the Value Chain Monitoring, the Sales Order Type "CBIC" (Intercompany Sales Order from Customer Order) will be assigned to the Value chain Category "ICSL - Intercompany Sales Process".

And the Customer Order "OR" should be assigned with the same Value Chain Category "ICSL" with Selling Company Process relevance and initiating Document check.

Path : SPRO > IMG > Sales and Distribution> Sales>Sales Document>Integration With Value Chain Monitoring>Assign Value Chain Category to Sales Document Type

Assign the item category to Value Chain Category

Path: SPRO > IMG > Sales and Distribution> Sales>Sales Document>Integration With Value Chain Monitoring>Assign Value Chain Category to Item Category

Assign the combination of Sales Document and Item Category to the Value Chain Category.

Path: SPRO > IMG > Sales and Distribution> Sales>Sales Document>Integration With Value Chain Monitoring>Assign Document type and Item Category to Value Chain

Assign the Tax Codes {C}

The intercompany Customer invoice will create the Supplier invoice for the intercompany Purchase order. The Tax Codes from the intercompany invoice (Sales relevant Tax Codes) will be mapped with the tax codes of the Supplier’s Invoice (Purchasing relevant Tax Codes).

Path: SPRO > IMG > Materials Management>Logistics Invoice Verification>Advanced intercompany process>Assign Tax codes for the transfer of supplier invoices

The process flow of the advanced intercompany process is in the second part of the Blog.

entry.title: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content:
Hi, 

so we have set up SAP Logistics Management and now want to try the Android and iOS Apps but we are running into serveral issues. 
First of all, we are using the "mobile Services" and not Work Zone, which shouldn't be an issue as I understand. 
Problem with iOS:
When opening the App SAP Warehouse Logistics I have to accept a licence agreement, when done so, I have to scan a QR Code. So far so good. Now I scan the QR Code and I'm getting a success notification which I confirm. After confirming the app sends me back to accepting the license agreement. So I'm caught in a loop and can't connect to SAP LGM. 
App Version: 1.1.0 --> no Update available
iOS Version: 26.3
Problem with Android:
After scanning the QR Code I can connect to SAP LGM and set up a PIN Code. After that I have to select a warehouse, but that is not possible. The app says "An error occured." There is a Button "try again" but when ein click it nothing happens. 
App Version: 1.1.0 --> no update available
Android Version: 13

Anyone else has the same problem? My guess is, that the problem is somehow connected to SAP Build Work Zone but I'm not sure why I should need it, as we use the Mobile Service.

entry.title: Connecting SAP Cloud ALM and SAP LeanIX - A step by step guide
entry.content:
This guide explains the clean, correct setup for integrating SAP Cloud ALM with SAP LeanIX. It covers both:

  • Landscape Discovery

  • Project Synchronization

Prerequisites

Before setting up the integration, make sure you have the following:

  • Admin rights in SAP LeanIX

  • Global administrator access to an SAP BTP cockpit that has the Cloud ALM API registered

  • S-user with the role Edit Cloud Data on SAP for Me.

    • You can manage your users and authorizations by following the steps described in Manage User Authorizations.

    • You're assigned to the customer number.

 

Step 1: Provision SAP Cloud ALM

  1. Request SAP Cloud ALM via SAP for Me
    Find a how to guide here: Requesting SAP CALM
  2. Setup SAP Cloud ALM
    Find a how to guide here: Required Setup for SAP Cloud ALM

Do not continue until this is confirmed working.


Step 2: Create the Service Key in SAP BTP

  1. Open the BTP subaccount hosting Cloud ALM.

  2. Navigate to Instances and Subscriptions and click create.

  3. Under Basic Info, provide the following details:
    • Service: SAP Cloud ALM API

    • Plan: standard

    • Runtime Environment: Other

    • Instance Name: Enter a meaningful instance name.

      The length of the instance name must not exceed 32 characters. Use only alphanumeric characters, numbers from 0 to 9, periods, and hyphens. Instead of spaces, use underscores.

  4. Choose Next
  5. Add the scopes
    To add the scopes, insert the following JSON and replace the relevant parameters with your instance name and the required scopes, respectively. The instance name must be the same one that you entered under Basic Info, in step 3.

    {
    "xs-security": {
    "xsappname": "<your-instance-name>",
    "authorities": [
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.landscape.read",
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.subscriptions.read",
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.ops-im-display-ui",
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.ops-im-configure-ui"
    ],
    "oauth2-configuration": {
    "credential-types": [
    "binding-secret"
    ]
    }
    }
    }

  6. Choose Create

Create Bindings

  1. Next to your instance, choose (Actions) and select Create Binding.

  2. Enter a name for your binding.
  3. Choose Create
  4. Next to the created binding, choose Actions and select View.

Result:

You can now see your service credentials in JSON format.

Important: You only need one service key, even if you configure both Discovery and Project Sync.

That’s all required on the BTP side.

For more information, please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/integrating-sap-leanix?locale=en-US


Step 3: Configure the Integration in LeanIX

In SAP LeanIX go to:

Administration → Integrations

You will configure two integrations separately, but both use the same service key JSON.


3.1 SAP Landscape Discovery

In the administration area of SAP LeanIX, navigate to Integrations.

    1. Choose Add Integrations
      .

    2. Locate SAP Landscape Discovery and choose Configure.

    3. Under Select Capabilities, select the primary capabilities that you want the integration to discover. This determines which SAP services and systems to include in the discovery process.

       

      Optional: Under Additional Capabilities, enable optional capabilities such as SAP system-level information and discovered flows. For more information, see Additional Capabilities.

    4. Copy the service key from the SAP BTP cockpit that you created in step 2 and paste it under Authenticate via API.

    5. Choose Save.

Once active, discovered SAP systems and services will appear in the Discovery Inbox for mapping to fact sheets.

To learn how to use the SAP Discovery inbox, please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/sap-landscape-discovery-inbox?locale=en-US


3.2 SAP Cloud ALM Project Sync

  1. In the administration area of SAP LeanIX, navigate to the Integrations section.

  2. Click Add Integrations.

  3. Locate SAP Cloud ALM for Managing Projects and click Configure.

  4. Download the service key from the SAP BTP cockpit that you previously created and input it under Authenticate via API.

  5. Click Save.

For more information and troubleshooting please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/configuring-sap-cloud-alm-integration-for-managing-projects?locale=en-US

What to expect:

  • Projects from Cloud ALM can synchronize to LeanIX

  • Updates from Cloud ALM flow into LeanIX

For more information, please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/sap-discovery-configuring-cloud-alm?locale=en-US


Result

After completing this setup, SAP LeanIX and SAP Cloud ALM operate as a connected system rather than isolated tools.

Your architecture repository reflects real SAP landscape data through automated discovery. Projects running in Cloud ALM become visible in LeanIX, creating transparency between transformation planning and execution. Stakeholders can trace initiatives, applications, and systems across the entire lifecycle.

The outcome is simple but powerful:

  • Architecture decisions are based on actual system data

  • Transformation roadmaps align with active projects

  • Execution progress feeds back into strategic planning

Instead of managing architecture and delivery in parallel worlds, you establish an integrated SAP toolchain that connects strategy, planning, and operations end-to-end.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercomapny Stock Transfer scenario and TM...
entry.content: The `T_TOR_EXE` authorization object checks are only for the **root execution organization**. It does **not** validate stage execution organizations in advanced intercompany STO scenarios. Each stage’s involved organizations need separate authorization handling if required.

entry.title: Re: Develop custom report in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @harshita_k10a126,

Point 1.

Option A: Embedded Analytics

Use this when you need KPIs, pivots, analytical lists, drilldowns:

  • Custom CDS Views
  • Custom Analytical Queries / Custom Analytical Queries and Services
  • Optional is Publish to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) or create a Multi-Dimensional Report from the query

SAP positions Custom CDS Views and Custom Analytical Queries exactly for this scenario

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/e30de6eae4d24d70b65996ac8ff88848.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/33f3a229ecfe438ca6b2053c48b4e1bb.html

Option B: Developer Extensibility ABAP Cloud/RAP

Use this if you need:

  • a fully custom UI/flow (custom selection logic, validations, actions)
  • complex logic that can’t be modeled in analytics
  • a custom OData service / Fiori elements app you control

This is SAP Developer Extensibility approach for Public Cloud

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e1059ff581854a699f15734049f14293.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e3527dfa1c5a41f69d4e63691f174f0a.html

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4036/4243/?utm_source=chatgpt.com&tab=overview

Point 2. Embedded Analytics step-by-step

Step 0: Check if SAP already delivers what you need

1. Open View Browser to explore CDS views/fields

2. Open Query Browser to search standard analytical queries

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2170')/S35

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1068')/S36

Step 1: Ensure the fields you need exist in the analytics sources

If you’re missing fields:

  • Add them via Custom Fields / in-app extensibility
  • Then make them available for reporting via Data Source Extensions this is the intended mechanism to retrieve additional fields SAP-delivered or custom fields into enabled data sources

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/52b845ed9a2e45b8bd556fc395340ad2.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/f71b07567568414face7043990335367.html

Step 2: Build your data source with Custom CDS Views

1. Open Custom CDS Views

2. Create a new view

3. Add fields/associations and follow modeling rules (keys, currency/unit semantics, correct joins

4. Save and publish

Optional (only if needed): You can also expose a Custom CDS View as an external API useful for consumption outside SAP

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b0000072fe994d7b9d5e015adf066059.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/0f2be3d26b7f48b0b0c426c188b55a89.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/ed003551d9c8456b914f7c94bd311b8a.html

Step 3: Create the report with Custom Analytical Queries

1. Open Custom Analytical Queries

2. Create a new query using your CDS view or copy an existing query and adjust

3. Define:

  • dimensions, measures
  • filters/parameters
  • calculated / restricted measures

4. Use Preview to validate results

Optional (very useful) enable runtime extensibility so business users can add additional fields at runtime without rebuilding the query

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/33f3a229ecfe438ca6b2053c48b4e1bb.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/4ef099d254cd4f8a96df001496f53952.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/25ed791bbae342b1bae8451744838cad.html

Step 4: Deliver it to users

In Public Cloud you typically don’t create brand-new catalogs you extend predelivered catalogs using Custom Catalog Extensions then assign roles. SAP documents this as the standard method

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/a58a58cf98584af6b4b1e6081b1cc6aa.html

Step 5: Transport DEV ->QAS ->PRD

Use:

  • Export Software Collection
  • Import Collection in the target system

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/6896de5f70c54753ac028f444eafa16f.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/74940689b4a54c89a04f11dc2247701a.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3126032

Point 3: Developer Extensibility when analytics isn’t enough

Step 1: Set up the ABAP development environment

  • install ABAP Development Tools (ADT)
  • create an ABAP Cloud project and connect to your system

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e3527dfa1c5a41f69d4e63691f174f0a.html

Step 2: Use only released APIs/objects (clean core)

SAP Developer Extensibility guidance is cloud-safe develop using released extension points/APIs/objects

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/using-developer-in-app-extensibility-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e1059ff581854a699f15734049f14293.html

To find released APIs:

  • use SAP Business Accelerator Hub

More details below:

https://api.sap.com/

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/1e60f14bdc224c2c975c8fa8bcfd7f3f.html

Step 3: Build the report app using RAP

RAP is the SAP-recommended model for modern ABAP applications (incl. Public Cloud)

More details from SAP Community:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/abap/rap

Step 4: Publish + authorize + transport

Point 4. Best practices:

  • Start from standard (View/Query Browser) and only build what’s missing
  • Prefer Key User analytics first it’s faster, upgrade-safe and intended for reporting
  • For ABAP, stick to released APIs/objects (clean core) to stay upgrade-safe
  • Design for performance keep joins minimal, always test with realistic volumes and enforce filters/parameters early
  • Treat publish to users as part of the deliverable tile + catalog extension + role assignment

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: TM - Create Stops in a Freight Order already created
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

 

I’m working on SAP TM and I’m trying to programmatically insert new Stops into an already created Freight Order using the following action:
    CREATE DATA lr_param.
    lr_param->log_loc_uuid = lv_uuid.
    lr_param->log_locid    = lv_ubip.

    lo_srv_tor->do_action(
      EXPORTING
        iv_act_key           = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_action-stop_successor-insert_before
        it_key               = lt_stage_first_key
        is_parameters        = lr_param

     IMPORTING
        eo_change            = lo_change
        eo_message           = lo_message
        et_failed_key        = lt_failed_key
        et_failed_action_key = lt_failed_action_key ).

The stop is created, but when I save the Freight Order I get the following error message:

"Validity of driver &1 is not within this time frame" (/SCMTMS/TOR 751)

I checked the Freight Order and noticed that the newly inserted stop is created without any planned dates, which seems to trigger the validity check on the assigned resource.

I found another action (stop-set_stop_dates) and tried to call it right after inserting the stop:

    CREATE DATA ls_parameters.
    LOOP AT lt_stage_first_key INTO DATA(la_stage_first_key).

      MOVE: la_stage_first_key-key TO la_parameter-stop_key,
            gv_start_date          TO la_parameter-carr_conf_start,
            gv_start_date          TO la_parameter-carr_conf_end.
      "'MA'                   TO la_parameter-dates_setby.",
*            gv_start_date          TO la_parameter-carr_conf_start.
      APPEND la_parameter TO ls_parameters->t_param. CLEAR la_parameter.

      MOVE la_stage_first_key-key TO la_key-key.
      APPEND la_key TO lt_key. CLEAR la_key.
    ENDLOOP.

    lo_srv_tor->do_action(
      EXPORTING
        iv_act_key           = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_action-stop-set_stop_dates
        it_key               = lt_stage_first_key
        is_parameters        = ls_parameters

     IMPORTING
        eo_change            = lo_change
        eo_message           = lo_message
        et_failed_key        = lt_failed_key.
Has anyone implemented stop creation + date assignment via /SCMTMS/CL_TOR_* actions and can advise how to correctly pass the parameters to the set_stop_dates action?

Any help or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you,
Eva

This behaviour is not expected. Of course USMD_RULE_SERVICE_CROSS_ET BAdI requires changed data to be triggered but you don't need to enter a value in Size/Dimension particularly to derive this field. I think there is some logic/overwrite issue in code.

I suggest you to analyse the implementation of USMD_RULE_SERVICE_CROSS_ET BAdI, also you might want to check the implementation in USMD_RULE_SERVICE BAdI. This BAdI imp may also overwrite this field at runtime.

For more information about derivations for material, you can check this link.

entry.title: Re: KE30.
entry.content:
KE30 (Profitability Report) only picks up values that flow through CO‑PA value fields. Direct postings from FI/MM (like FB50 or MIRO) don’t automatically appear unless they are transferred into CO‑PA via the correct account assignment and valuation logic.

How to Fix

Check PA Transfer Structure (KEI1/KEI2): Ensure the cost elements used in FI/MM postings are mapped to the correct value fields.

Check Account Assignment: Make sure the FI/MM postings carry a profitability segment (e.g., customer, product). Without this, CO‑PA won’t record the values.

Validate Costing Keys: Confirm that the cost classes assigned to cost groups are correctly tied to the intended value fields.

Run Reconciliation (KEAT): Compare FI/MM postings with CO‑PA to see which postings are missing and why.

entry.title: BW Modernisation with the SAP BW Data Product Generator
entry.content:
The DataProduct Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud in SAP BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit

With the "SAP BW/4HANA DataProduct Generator for SAP BDC" a functionality is availabe as part of the BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit to expose existing SAP BW/4HANA artifacts ( i.e. Advanced DataStore Objects, CompositeProviders and Queries as an InfoProvider) as local tables to the Object Store. For SAP BW 7.5 there is a SAP GUI based UI available. In a second step the Data Sharing Cockpit of Datasphere can be used to create DataProducts out of such local tables.

1) Activities in the Data Provisioning Cockpit:

Scenario: The underlying Scenario of this chapter is to create a DataProduct out of an Advanced DataStore Object from a SAP BW/4HANA system in the private Cloud which containes additional CashFlow data. This data should be made available as a DataProduct to BDC to be merged with the existing Working Capital data from the exercises before.

Update March 2026: Since the availability of SAP BW/4HANA Modeling Tools in Eclipse 1.27 there is an additional way to move a BW/4HANA InfoProvider to the Object Store of SAP Datasphere (as a basis for the Data Product generation). As a result, a subscription is generated. A data subscription is a new BW object type that allows you to extract data from ODP-based sources within your BW system into local tables (file) in the SAP Datasphere system of your SAP Business Data Cloud formation. In the following chapters both options are described:

1.1) Exposing an InfoProvider of SAP BW/4HANA via the Data Product Generator (in SAP BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit)

  • Establish a subscription in SAP BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit, select the InfoProvider which needs to be exposed to the ObjectStore and activate the subscription:

(remark: "CF in thge description of the objects in the screenshots is referring to the scenario of the exercise and means "Cash Flow")

  • Regular transfers can be scheduled from the subscription using a BW Process Chain. With every execution of the BW Process Chain the data is pushed into the SAP Datasphere object store. There is a new Process Type available to be used to propagate the data of an InfoProvider to the ObjectStore "Load Process and Postprocessing - Data Subscription":

The status of the latest execution of a subscription can be checked in the subscription overview:

For each subscription the local table can be viewed as well as its content. By pressing "View Local Table" for a given subscription you will be guided to the Datasphere instance and the respective local table in the Data Builder. From here the content of the table can also be checked.

1.2) Exposing an InfoProvider of SAP BW/4HANA via the Scenario Generator (with the SAP BW/4HANA Modeling Tools in Eclipse)

With the SAP BW/4HANA Modelling Tools in Eclipse 1.27 there is an additional possibility to expose a BW InfoProvider to the Object Store of SAP Datasphere. Eitherway, regardless which option is used, the local table (subscription) has to be used as a basis in the Data Sharing Cockpit to generate a Data Product.

There are three important concepts which neeed to be considered using the Scenario Generator:

A 'scenario' is a list of references between data subscriptions which are required to reconstruct a specific BW model (such as a DataStore object (advanced) (aDSO) or a CompositeProvider (HCPR)). For example, if you have a CompositeProvider, you would not only need a data subscription to represent the top-level object, but also further data subscriptions for the InfoObjects it contains and other dependencies.

The 'scenario tree' saves these dependencies between the objects in the BW model, as well as the equivalent data subscriptions and their dependencies. It also considers existing data subscriptions for dependent objects to avoid the creation of duplicate data subscriptions and local tables (file).

The 'Scenario Generator' lets you not only create scenarios out of your BW models, but also mass generate the associated data subscriptions out of the scenario tree. Through this you can replicate an entire BW model into local tables (file) in your SAP Datasphere tenant in SAP Business Data Cloud. There, the extracted data and associated semantics are stored in table format and become available for further use cases, such as creating data products or sharing to SAP Databricks.

So the basic workflow in the Scenario Generator is:

a) To create a Scenario which is a collection of all Objects belonging to a given BW InfoProvider and their dependencies. For example a CompositeProvider alone does not contain any data. The data is coming from its PartProviders so a scenario of a CompositeProvider would also include the PartProviders.

b) Generate the subscriptions which consists of the lokal tables and the mechanisms to replicate the data from the InfoProviders to the local tables

As a basis for the Data Product to be created this time the CompositeProvider (DPG_CP_CF) from the scenario above is taken. In the Scenario Generators first tab 'Scenario Core Objects' some general settings can be made before collecting the objects related to a selected InfoProvider to create the scenario and the dependency tree of all objects belonging to the scenario.

  • 'Selection Grouping' defines which related objects shoud be collected for a selected BW InfoProvider. It can be specified that 'only necessary' objects are taken into consideration or also the Upstream and downstream objects along the Dataflow of the InfoProvider.
  • The 'Association Levels' define the amount of levels included in the dependency tree.
  • There are some optional checkboxes to activate the possibility to transport subscriptions or to consider already existing subscriptions to prevent duplicates and redundant subscriptions on objects where a subscription already exists.

After pressing the 'Collect' Button, all dependent objects from the CompositeProvider are collected which are the Inbound ADSO as the only PartProvider and the second Object collected is the InfoObject 'Company Code' which is the only real InfoObject in the Inbound ADSO (rest of the columns are fieldbased).

After the collection of the objects the relationsships are displayed in the Tab 'Object Relations'. In this list all subscriptions which are generated in this second step can be seen and the generation of the subscription triggered. After this the 'Message' tab is shown where all relevant messages from the generation process can be seen.

2) Creation of a DataProduct out of the exposed SAP BW/4HANA ADSO

To create a DataProduct from the local table the Data Sharing Studio from Datasphere must be used. In a BDC Formation there can only be one Datasphere System assigned to the BW 7.5 or BW/4HANA instance.

Before the DataProduct can be generated, a Data Provider Profile must be defined (or an existing one used) where the mandatory flag "Data Provider Visibility" is set to "Unified Customer Landscape Formations".

Now a new DataProduct can be created (Data Sharing Cockpit > My Data Products > Create Product)

In the definition of the DataProduct the fields for the Contexts and Deliverymode are automatically taken from the Data Provider Profile and to connect to the right ADSO the corresponding Datasphere Space needs to be specified where the ADSO is located:

After saving the DataProduct it should be available in the overview table under "My Data Products". By selecting this entry in the table the Status can be switched to "listed".

3) Next Steps

Optional: create a graphical view to bring the data of this newly created DataProduct together with the Working Capital / Chash Flow data which is already available from the exercises before.

You can also now continue with the reading material that is provided about accessing Data products from customized S/4HANA CDS views.

4) Important Links / Notes

Pre-requisites

  • SAP Note [3590400 - Configuration SAP BW and SAP BW/4HANA: Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud] (https://me.sap.com/notes/3590400)

  • SAP Note [3590318 Pilot BW Configuration for Data Product Generator in SAP BDC] (https://me.sap.com/notes/3590318)

  • SAP Note [3593519, TCI2 for BW on HANA, BW/4HANA] (https://me.sap.com/notes/3593519)

  • SAP Note 3581186 - OA2C: RFC API

  • SAP Notes for Transaction SOAUTH2_CLIENT:

  • 3532137 - OA2C: Dynpro UI for OAuth2.0 Client Configuration

  • 3561719 - OA2C: New dynpro UI SOAUTH2_CLIENT, generation of DDIC object

  • 3561640 - OA2C: Token Status for SOAUTH2_CLIENT

  • 3692414 - Data Subscription TLOGO: After TCI 3.0

  • Supported Releases of SAP BW 7.5 / SAP BW/4HANA:

  • SAP BW 7.50 SP24 onwards

  • SAP BW/4HANA 2021 SP4 onwards

  • SAP BW/4HANA 2023 SP0 onwards

entry.title: SAP BO - Preventing values from beeing written as clear text in .wid files when saving the report
entry.content:
Hello

We are using SAP BO  WebI and currently have a huge compliance problem, because values from the report and also from the search/queries (let's say customer_phone_number) land as clear text in the .wid file when the report is saved. When opening the .wid file with the text editor, half is gibberish but there are also clear client data in that text e.g. the number.

What would save us:

  • If everything could be rendered gibberish in the .wid file
  • If SAP BO could encrypt the .wid file (if we encrypt it using other applications then SAP BO can't read it anymore)
  • If SAP BO would have a possibility to store these files in the DB instead of on the filesystem
  • If there would be some event/hook when SAP BO tries to load the file, we could have a script which downlaods the file from the database and uploads when not needed

Or maybe you have another idea? So the goal is, that client data can be queried and shown in the report but when saved it must not land in the .wid file or that file needs to be unreadable to other users when on disk.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Bank Statement Item Not Marked as Completed After Automatic Clearing
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

When I perform an automatic clearing of a payment, the system correctly links the operation to the corresponding bank statement item. Up to this point everything works as expected. However, when I later check that bank statement item, the line still appears as “Not Completed”, even though the clearing has been successfully executed.

In order for the item to be marked as completed, I have to manually change the status within the bank statement itself, which does not seem consistent with a fully automated process.

My questions are:

  • Is there any standard way for the system to automatically update the status of the bank statement item once the automatic clearing has been executed?

  • Is there any additional configuration for the item to be marked as completed without manual intervention?

  • Is this behavior a standard limitation in SAP Public Cloud when the clearing is performed outside the bank statement processing flow?

I appreciate any guidance or experiences you can share.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Improve Performance of TM integration of Sales Scheduling Agreements
entry.content:
Dear friends of TM,

Scheduling agreements cover the supply of goods from a supplier to a customer. It’s very common that they are valid for a longer period, often several years. That means that they are growing and growing in terms of their schedule lines, their document flow etc.

Now, there are means to reduce complexity, i.e. transaction SACO for sales scheduling agreements. But nevertheless, in TM integration this can create big challenges as we need to consider every single schedule line to calculate the open quantity still to be delivered, identify the transport requirements and built freight units.

Luckily, we meanwhile have some medicine for this headache. With release S/4HANA 2023 we released a customizing setting for purchase scheduling agreements, where customers can create rules to define which schedule lines should be considered during TM integration without loosing any functionality and still creating the correct freight units for the transport requirements. With release S/4HANA 2025 FPS1, we now also extended this to sales scheduling agreements.

Let’s take a look to what this means in detail.

Concept of the future horizon

At first, we need to ask the question which schedule lines are actually relevant for TM integration when looking into the future . If I’m the customer, I might send the releases to my supplier one year in advance. But when I’m responsible for parts of the transport…do I really plan those transports one year in advance? Maybe it’s only a couple of weeks in advance…and here the customizable rules come into play. With those, I can dynamically define how many schedule lines should be considered.

And dynamically means:

  • I define a timeframe in which schedule lines should be counted.
  • Depending on the count, I can define the horizon which should be considered during TM integration and thus how many schedule lines should be considered
  • Basic paradigm: The more schedule lines I count in the defined time frame, the shorter is the horizon of schedule lines to be considered. Or in other words: The higher the density of schedule lines is, the shorter is the horizon to be considered.

Let’s make it more clear with an example:

In the upper part, the density of schedule lines in the defined time frame is lower than in the lower part. Therefore, the end date for considering schedule lines in the upper part is more in the future.

Those rules can be defined for purchase and sales scheduling agreements.

Example

The definition of the rules are with the means of a rule group. This can be defined in customizing in the logistics integration part:

And the rule groups then are assigned to the logistics integration profile:

Now, the rule groups are considered every time the scheduling agreement is integrated with TM

Report / Transaction

There are also very rare cases where a scheduling agreement is not integrated with TM after a change (i.e. update with new releases).

For those cases we have created report /SCMTMS/UPDATE_INT_TIMERANGE where the new end date for considering schedule lines of a purchase scheduling agreement can be calculated.

This report has been extended for sales scheduling agreements and with some additional functions e.g., creating freight units:

For more information see also the release note.

So, with this innovation, you can manage your schedule agreements much more efficiently and you can improve the performance in TM integration a lot!

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Can co-products in SAP ECC have independent serial nu...
entry.content:
Your PP colleague is not entirely correct — co‑products can indeed have serial numbers in SAP. The limitation is not about the co‑product scenario itself, but about how serial number management is configured and used in production postings.

Key Points

Co‑product scenario (PP/CO):
You define co‑products in the BOM and routing. When you confirm production, the system posts goods receipt for the main product and co‑products proportionally. Costs are split according to apportionment.

Serial number management:
Serial numbers are controlled at the material master level (MM view). If a co‑product material is flagged as “serial number managed,” the system will require serial numbers during goods receipt, even in a co‑product posting. This applies regardless of whether the material is a main product, co‑product, or by‑product.

Traceability:
Each co‑product can have its own independent serial number profile. During production order confirmation (CO11N or Fiori equivalent), the system will prompt for serial numbers for each co‑product material. This ensures full traceability.

Where the confusion comes from
In some implementations, co‑products are treated as “joint products” without individual tracking, so serial numbers aren’t maintained. If serial number management is not activated in the material master, then yes, you cannot trace them. But technically, SAP does not block serial number assignment for co‑products.

These new releases include fully corrected and revalidated hands-on exercises on the S4U SAP S/4HANA 2025 demo system, with master data and configuration aligned to the 2025 tenant.

A hands-on course dedicated to logistics, maintenance processes, and operational readiness in Defense & Security environments.

entry.title: SAP expands delivery of SAP Learning Class to meet growing needs of digital workforce
entry.content:
SAP offers learners a variety of ways for learners to consume content, from free access through to certification. One of the preferred ways to learn is using SAP Learning Class. This premium scheduled, instructor-led SAP training offering is delivered by SAP or SAP Authorized Education Partners and helps learners build practical SAP skills using real-life scenarios. 

Courses follow SAP-defined learning content and best practices, are delivered live by SAP-certified instructors, and are available in-person or virtually, with hands-on, certification-relevant content.

In 2026, SAP is expanding how learners access this instructor-led training by extending the partner-managed delivery model for SAP Learning Class courses, with a growing number of courses being delivered by SAP-certified instructors from partners.

SAP continues to own, author, update, and govern all course content and live training systems, while partners schedule, price, and deliver the courses. This expanded delivery model increases learner choice, flexibility, time-zone and language coverage, and course availability, while maintaining the premium learning experience associated with SAP Learning Class.

Expanding access to premium instructor-led training supports SAP’s pledge to upskill 12 million people with AI-ready skills by 2030.

Learn more about SAP Learning Class and the available courses.  

#SAPLearningClass #SAPLearning #SAPCertification #InstructorLedTraining #AIReadySkills

entry.title: Multistage Intercompany Scenario - Logistics Integration
entry.content:
Dear friends of TM,

Large enterprises usually contain of multiple entities, especially when they operate international. For instance, they contain separate companies for selling, delivering, and other purposes.

With S/4HANA2022, we already delivered the TM integration in the Advanced Intercompany Sales / Stock Transfer Scenario where the shipping enterprise had separate selling and delivering companies.

With S/4HANA2025 FPS01, we now also have the Multistage Intercompany Scenarios, where besides the selling and delivering company, multiple intermediate companies can be involved.

This blog posts talks about the respective TM integration of Multistage Intercompany Scenarios and its logistical aspects. Those are the freight unit stage building according to incoterms between the companies and also the updates to the outbound delivery with planned and actual dates from the supply chain.

Scenario Overview

Below is an exemplary Multistage Intercompany Sales Scenario with two intermediate companies:

Here, a customer places an order at the enterprise with the incoterm DDP which makes the enterprise responsible for the whole transport to the customer.

Within the enterprise, we have:

  • A selling company 1710 which sells the cargo to the customer.
  • A delivering company 1010 from which the cargo is delivered.
  • The intermediate companies 2210 and 2410.
  • The incoterms for the transfer of responsibilities between the companies are FAS (at the port of loading) and CIF (at the port of unloading).
Logistic Integration

The respective sales order looks as follows:

  • The customer is business partner 17100002
  • The incoterm DDP towards the customer is mentioned whereas the incoterm location is the customer’s address
  • The product is sold from sales area 1710 of company 1710.

The decision about the value chain and the affected companies is made on item level:

The cargo is delivered from plant 1010 of company 1010 with involvement of plant 1710 of selling company 1710. This defines the value chain.

The actual value chain is built with the sales and purchase orders as shown in the above scenario overview. So, after the initiating sales order towards the customer is created, the sales and purchase orders between the companies are created as well.

If the initiating sales order is relevant for TM, logistics integration collects the incoterms of the ‘Intercompany’ purchase orders, which are usually derived from respective info records.

And according to the incoterms and incoterm locations, TM then identifies the transport demand and builds the freight unit with its stages:

  • Pre-carriage from the delivering plant in company 1010 until the port of loading (in company 2410)
  • Main leg from the port of loading to the port of unloading (in company 1710)
  • On-carriage to the customer

In the picture above, transport planning has already happened with respective freight documents. With that, it’s clear when the cargo is planned to arrive at the incoterm location and thus when the responsibility is planned to be handed over from one company to the next one.

This is called ‘Transfer of Control (ToC)’ and the dates are stored as ‘planned ToC dates’ in the outbound delivery assigned to the sales order.

  • External Transfer of Control date for the delivery to the customer
  • Internal Transfer of Control for the responsibility transfer between the companies

When then the transport is executed, respective events (or transport activities) indicate when the transfer of responsibilities happened. These dates are again transferred to the outbound delivery as ‘actual ToC dates’:

The Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer Scenario is very similar. The only difference is that there’s no customer but a receiving entity within the enterprise instead.

Summary

Please see here a brief overview of the scope of te TM integration and the huge benefits customers have when they integrate this scenario with TM:

And here is the respective release note.

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Cannot Select Today for New Schedule
entry.content:
Good day @MuratTnrkt 

Thank you for your question.

Question: Is this a standard behavior where schedules can only be created for future periods rather than the current day?

Answer: Yes, it is. In Resource Scheduling, a schedule is a simulation until it is frozen. Any schedules that have not been frozen before their start date are automatically deleted. Because of this, the manual popup likely restricts start date selection to future dates to prevent you from creating a schedule that would immediately enter its start period (and potentially be deleted if you do not freeze it).

See the references below for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/3a8e781161f744fa8361014455b0fe9e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/f2d1705780175c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-business-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-asset-management/exploring-resource-scheduling_c29ff603-085e-4350-9c27-1037fe8e0f04-1

Regarding you point on “Also in the Generate Recurring Schedules app, the system allows selecting 'Today' as the start date with single, creates a schedule but it fails to show in manage schedule app;”

This can happen because the two apps are acting at different layers:

  • Generate Recurring Schedules validates the job scheduling input. An example is a Single Run that start today.
  • The generated schedule is still subject to RSH schedule lifecycle rules. This includes deletion if it not frozen before start date.

So, the behaviour you see is in line with a scenario where, schedule is generated, schedule period starts immediately, schedule is not frozen and schedule is then deleted.

Regarding you point on enhancement being a good idea to allow Today in manual schedule creation, you are right to be cautious about the impact on Freeze and Execution phase logic. Allowing Today in the manual create popup through enhancement could conflict with the intended process logic, especially around freeze timing, execution phase start and automatic deletion of non frozen schedule. This can create confusion for planners and destabilise your operational behaviour. Since there are no official details in public cloud on that, I recommend sticking to standard behaviour.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP Back Replication of Packaging Fees
entry.content:
This is a good and nuanced question because you’re dealing with two different replication targets in S/4HANA Cloud: material classification mapping (for attributes like kg of non‑recycled plastic) and pricing condition mapping (for amounts like fees/taxes).

1. Replicating kg of Non‑Recycled Plastic
This is already well‑documented:

  • You map the quantity attribute from the replication API to a material classification characteristic (e.g., ATNAM/ATWRT in the iDoc).
  • This allows the invoice form to print the aggregated kg per product.

Correct approach: Material Classification Mapping.

2. Replicating Plastic Tax (Total Fee)
Here you have two options depending on how you want to consume the data:

Option A: Material Classification Mapping
You can treat the TotalFee attribute as another classification characteristic. In the iDoc structure, this would be mapped into E1CUVAL / ATWRT (characteristic value) under the material classification segment. This way, the fee is stored as an informational attribute, not affecting pricing.

Advantage: Keeps everything in classification, consistent with the kg mapping.
Limitation: You cannot use condition techniques (like subtotals) because it’s not a condition.

Option B: SD Pricing Condition Mapping
You replicate the fee into a dedicated condition type (e.g., ZPLT for Plastic Tax). In customizing, you mark this condition as statistical (so it does not affect net value). It will then appear in the invoice condition list, purely for printing.

Advantage: Standard way to show amounts on invoices.
Limitation: Requires condition type setup and mapping in the SD integration.

3. Which Approach to Use
If the requirement is purely informational (no pricing, no totals):
→ Use Material Classification Mapping. Map TotalFee → ATWRT in E1CUVAL.

If the requirement is to show the fee explicitly in the invoice condition list (like other surcharges/taxes):
→ Use SD Pricing Condition Mapping with a statistical condition type.

4. Practical Recommendation
Many clients choose Option B (SD condition mapping) for fees/taxes because:

  • It integrates naturally into the invoice condition list.
  • It’s easier to print in standard invoice forms (conditions are already available).
  • You can keep it statistical so it doesn’t affect pricing.

Meanwhile, Option A (classification) is best for non‑monetary attributes like kg of plastic.

entry.title: Re: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
@alexey_arseniev, thank you for the comment.

I knew IF_SXML_READER/IF_SXML_WRITER, and now looked at IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER. But they are like "middleware" serializers, with which you need to write for every API data, ABAP mapping code. I checked also XCO_JSON in the past, an it is much slower even than /UI2/* one.

These serializations look more like transformations, where instead of maintaining transformations for each API type, you need to maintain ABAP code for mapping for each API type. And ABAP mapping will be even slower than tranformation.

Maybe I confused with "ABAP Kernel" term. What I wanted to mean is ABAP language implementation itself. I thought it is also ABAP Kernel, but maybe not, I am not sure.

Regarding ABAP types in "kernel". E.g. CALL TRANSFORMATION id can map dirrectly to ABAP types (maybe even generate references), but it supports only asJSON. The main question is why something like that cannot exist for "normal" JSON?
